guarantee: An access token whose backing AuthSession has been revoked (revokedAt set) is rejected by the guards on /me and /resend-verification with 401 {code:'UNAUTHORIZED'} and request.user is never populated.
testTitle: authGuard rejects access tokens whose backing session has been revoked

guarantee: Guards derive request.user (organisationId, role) from the current DB row, not from stale JWT claims, so a token minted with an old org/role cannot act with those stale claims.
testTitle: authGuard uses current database role and organisation instead of stale JWT claims

guarantee: Refresh rotation atomically revokes the presented session and issues exactly one replacement; if the conditional revoke updates 0 rows (lost race), rotation fails with 401 INVALID_REFRESH_TOKEN and NO replacement AuthSession is created.
testTitle: refresh token rotation fails without creating a replacement session when revoke loses the race

guarantee: Replaying an already-revoked refresh token (theft detection) is rejected with 401 INVALID_REFRESH_TOKEN AND triggers revocation of all of that user's active sessions, without looking up the user or creating a new session.
testTitle: refresh token replay revokes active sessions for the affected user

guarantee: Access tokens are signed/verified with pinned HS256 and bound to the charitypilot-api issuer and charitypilot-web audience; tokens using a different algorithm, audience, or missing issuer are rejected.
testTitle: access tokens are signed and verified with the pinned HS256 algorithm

guarantee: resetPassword consumes the reset token atomically (conditional updateMany) before revoking all active sessions; a concurrently-consumed token (count 0) fails with 400 INVALID_RESET_TOKEN and sessions are NOT revoked.
testTitle: resetPassword consumes the token, sets the password, and revokes all sessions

guarantee: verifyEmail is single-use: a valid token marks the account verified, and a token consumed concurrently (updateMany count 0) fails with 400 INVALID_VERIFY_TOKEN without an unconditional update.
testTitle: verifyEmail marks the account verified when the token is valid

guarantee: On successful POST /login the route sets the auth (access + refresh) cookies via setAuthCookies and returns only publicUser (no passwordHash); a 200 response carries a Set-Cookie header.
gapDescription: No test injects a successful /login at the route level to assert Set-Cookie is issued and the body omits passwordHash. login() service success is exercised only indirectly; cookie issuance is untested for the happy path.
proposedTitle: login route issues auth cookies and returns the public user without secrets
proposedApproach: Boot Fastify({logger:false}); decorate prisma with user.findUnique returning a user whose passwordHash is bcrypt.hashSync('NewPassword1',12), plus authSession.create returning {id:'s1'}; register authRoutes at /auth; inject POST /auth/login with the matching credentials; assert 200, response.headers['set-cookie'] is defined, and response.json().user has no passwordHash field.

guarantee: Secrets and tokens (authorization header, cookies, body.password, body.refreshToken, accessToken, provider keys) are redacted in production logs and never appear in serialized error output.
testTitle: production logger redacts browser credentials, provider secrets, and auth tokens

guarantee: Each Zod-validated auth endpoint (register, login, forgot-password, reset-password, verify-email) rejects a malformed/missing-field body with HTTP 400 {code:'VALIDATION_ERROR'} and a structured details array, never a 500 or stack leak.
gapDescription: No test injects a malformed body to any auth endpoint to assert the 400 VALIDATION_ERROR shape. auth-throttling.test.ts and request-origin.test.ts only send well-formed bodies (asserting 200/400-token/401), so the formatZodError branch (route index.ts lines 45-48 etc.) is unproven.
proposedTitle: auth endpoints reject malformed bodies with a 400 VALIDATION_ERROR and never 500
proposedApproach: Boot Fastify({logger:false}); decorate prisma with user.findUnique/findFirst returning null (writes must never run); register authRoutes at /auth; table-drive inject POST to /auth/register (missing password), /auth/login (email:'not-an-email'), /auth/forgot-password ({}), /auth/reset-password ({token:''}), /auth/verify-email ({}); for each assert statusCode 400, json().code === 'VALIDATION_ERROR', and json().details is an array.

guarantee: register rejects passwords failing the strength policy (min 8, upper/lower/digit) and over-length name/organisationName with 400 VALIDATION_ERROR, never starting the org/user-creation transaction.
gapDescription: registerSchema enforces password complexity and field length caps, but no test asserts a weak password or oversized field yields 400 VALIDATION_ERROR with the $transaction mock proven NOT called.
proposedTitle: register rejects weak passwords without starting the creation transaction
proposedApproach: Boot Fastify({logger:false}); decorate prisma with user.findUnique async()=>null and $transaction async()=>{transactionCalled=true;}; register authRoutes at /auth; inject POST /auth/register with password 'alllowercase' (no upper/digit); assert 400, json().code==='VALIDATION_ERROR', and assert transactionCalled === false.

guarantee: When the email provider fails to send the verification email, POST /resend-verification returns a clean 503 {code:'EMAIL_DELIVERY_FAILED'} (from resendEmailVerification when sendEmailVerification resolves falsy) rather than a 200 or 500.
gapDescription: auth.service.ts lines 248-251 throw AppError(503,'EMAIL_DELIVERY_FAILED') when the send returns falsy, but every existing test mocks sendEmailVerification to return true, so the 503 degradation branch is unproven.
proposedTitle: resendEmailVerification surfaces a 503 when the verification email cannot be sent
proposedApproach: Instantiate new AuthService(prismaMock, emailMock) where prisma.user.findUnique returns {id:'u1',email:'o@e.org',name:'O',emailVerified:false} and user.update resolves, and emailMock.sendEmailVerification async()=>false; assert.rejects(service.resendEmailVerification('u1')) with err.statusCode===503 && err.code==='EMAIL_DELIVERY_FAILED'.

guarantee: register does not disclose that an email is already registered: an existing email yields 202 with the neutral REGISTRATION_ACCEPTED_MESSAGE, no Set-Cookie, and no org/user creation transaction is started.
testTitle: register route does not disclose duplicate emails or issue session cookies

guarantee: register also flattens the DB-enforced unique-email race: when the creation transaction throws a Prisma P2002 unique-constraint error, the service returns the same neutral accepted message instead of leaking the conflict or 500ing.
gapDescription: auth.service.ts lines 119-125 catch isUniqueConstraintError(P2002) from the $transaction and return registrationAccepted(); the analogous race is tested for team invites but not for register, so the register P2002 path is unproven.
proposedTitle: register flattens a raced duplicate-email unique-constraint failure to the neutral message
proposedApproach: Instantiate new AuthService(prismaMock, emailMock) where user.findUnique returns null (passes the pre-check) and $transaction throws new Prisma.PrismaClientKnownRequestError('...',{code:'P2002',clientVersion:'test'}); assert the result message matches /check your email/i and emailMock.sent.length === 0 (no verification email for the raced duplicate).

guarantee: forgot-password is enumeration-safe: it returns the same generic message for both known and unknown accounts, storing a hashed (sha256) reset token only for a known account and sending nothing for an unknown one.
testTitle: forgotPassword sends a reset link for a known account and returns the generic message

guarantee: login is enumeration-safe: an unknown email returns the generic 401 INVALID_CREDENTIALS ('Invalid email or password') and the no-user path still spends a full bcrypt comparison so latency does not reveal account existence.
testTitle: login returns the generic INVALID_CREDENTIALS error for unknown accounts

guarantee: Sensitive auth endpoints (forgot-password, reset-password, verify-email, resend-verification) enforce per-route throttling stricter than the global limit, returning 429 on the 6th request within the window.
testTitle: forgot-password has route-specific throttling below the global limit

guarantee: The two non-public auth endpoints require a valid authenticated identity: an unauthenticated POST /resend-verification is rejected with 401 {code:'UNAUTHORIZED'} before any service work; the same authIdentityGuard protects GET /me.
testTitle: resend-verification has route-specific throttling below the global limit
gapDescription: The throttling test confirms unauthenticated /resend-verification returns 401 UNAUTHORIZED, but there is no explicit guard test for GET /me rejecting a missing/invalid token, and no positive test that an authenticated /me returns the caller's own public user. authIdentityGuard internals are covered generically in auth-isolation.test.ts but not wired through the /me route.
proposedTitle: GET /me requires authentication and returns only the caller's public user
proposedApproach: Boot Fastify({logger:false}); decorate prisma with authSession.findFirst async()=>({id:'s1'}) and user.findUnique returning the caller row; register authRoutes at /auth; inject GET /auth/me with no Authorization header asserting 401 {code:'UNAUTHORIZED'}, then inject with a valid Bearer signAccessToken({userId,organisationId,role,sessionId}) asserting 200 and that json() has no passwordHash.

guarantee: Cross-organisation resource isolation: a user in org A cannot read or mutate an org-B resource through any auth endpoint.
gapDescription: The auth route group operates exclusively on the caller's own identity: getMe and resendEmailVerification key on request.user.userId (set by the guard from the verified session), while login/forgotPassword/resetPassword/verifyEmail key on a self-supplied email or an unguessable single-use sha256-hashed token. None of these endpoints take an org-scoped resource id from the client, so there is no sibling resource to leak across orgs. Cross-org guard behaviour (org derived from current DB row) is itself covered in auth-isolation.test.ts.

guarantee: Subscription/plan gating (subscriptionGuard, requireCompletePlan) on auth endpoints.
gapDescription: No auth endpoint registers subscriptionGuard or requireCompletePlan; /me and /resend-verification use only authIdentityGuard, and the remaining endpoints are public. register deliberately provisions a TRIALING ESSENTIALS subscription rather than gating on one. Plan-gating therefore does not apply to this route group.

guarantee: GET / returns ONLY the authenticated caller's organisation: the service calls organisation.findUnique with where.id === request.user.organisationId and never with any client-supplied id, so a user in org A can never read org B's organisation record.
testTitle: getOrganisation throws when the organisation does not exist
gapDescription: The not-found path (org === null -> ORG_NOT_FOUND) is proven, but no test asserts that getOrganisation passes request.user.organisationId straight into findUnique({where:{id}}) — i.e. that the scoping id is the caller's org and not attacker-controllable. Because the route exposes no id param, this is the key isolation invariant and it is currently only implied, not asserted.
proposedTitle: getOrganisation scopes the lookup to the caller's organisationId
proposedApproach: Use the existing orgPrisma() helper but capture the findUnique args; call service.getOrganisation('org_1') and assert the recorded organisation.findUnique call had args.where.id === 'org_1'. This proves the only id used is the caller's, with no path-param injection vector.

guarantee: PATCH / writes ONLY to the caller's organisation: organisation.update is always called with where.id === request.user.organisationId, so a user in org A can never mutate org B's organisation record.
testTitle: updateOrganisation regenerates auto-deadlines when the financial year end changes
gapDescription: Existing tests prove the deadline-regen side-effect logic but never assert that organisation.update's where.id equals the organisationId argument passed in. The scoping invariant on the write itself is unproven.
proposedTitle: updateOrganisation scopes the update to the caller's organisationId
proposedApproach: With orgPrisma() recording calls, call service.updateOrganisation('org_1', { name: 'New Name' }); find the recorded 'organisation.update' call and assert (args as {where:{id:string}}).where.id === 'org_1'. No cross-org id can be supplied because the route has no id param.

guarantee: The GET/PATCH response body never leaks fields outside the publicOrganisation allow-list (id, name, rcnNumber, croNumber, legalForm, complexity, charitablePurpose, financialYearEnd, registeredAddress, contactEmail, contactPhone, website, dateRegistered, lastAgmDate) — i.e. no internal/cross-tenant columns are returned.
gapDescription: No test asserts that the serialised organisation response is restricted to publicOrganisationSelect / publicOrganisation keys; a regression adding a sensitive column to the select would go undetected.
proposedTitle: GET / returns only the public organisation field allow-list
proposedApproach: Boot Fastify, decorate prisma with authModels(OWNER, activeSubscription) plus organisation.findUnique returning an object that also includes a forbidden field (e.g. stripeCustomerId), register organisationRoutes, inject GET / with a Bearer OWNER token, expect 200 and assert Object.keys(res.json().data) deep-equals the publicOrganisation key set (no extra keys).

guarantee: PATCH / rejects a MEMBER with 403 {code:'FORBIDDEN'} via requireAdmin and never calls organisation.update.
gapDescription: requireAdmin is proven for the board-member PATCH/POST/DELETE routes in route-guards.test.ts, but NOT for organisationRoutes; no test registers this plugin and asserts a MEMBER PATCH is blocked before the write.
proposedTitle: a MEMBER cannot PATCH the organisation (requireAdmin)
proposedApproach: Boot Fastify, decorate prisma with authModels('MEMBER', activeSubscription()) plus an organisation.update that flips a updateCalled flag, register organisationRoutes, inject PATCH / with a MEMBER Bearer token and payload { name: 'X' }; assert statusCode 403, json().code === 'FORBIDDEN', and updateCalled === false.

guarantee: PATCH / is permitted for ADMIN and OWNER (requireAdmin = requireRole('OWNER','ADMIN')), reaching the service and returning 200.
gapDescription: No positive-path test proves ADMIN/OWNER can PATCH the organisation through this plugin; only board-member ADMIN create is proven elsewhere.
proposedTitle: an ADMIN may PATCH the organisation
proposedApproach: Boot Fastify with authModels('ADMIN', activeSubscription()) and organisation.update returning a public org shape, register organisationRoutes, inject PATCH / with an ADMIN Bearer token and payload { name: 'Renamed' }; assert statusCode 200 and that organisation.update was called.

guarantee: GET / does NOT require admin: a MEMBER with an active subscription can read the organisation (read is gated only by authGuard+subscriptionGuard, not requireAdmin).
gapDescription: No test proves the asymmetry that read is open to MEMBER while write is admin-only on this plugin (analogous to the board-member 'a MEMBER can still read' test, which does not cover organisationRoutes).
proposedTitle: a MEMBER can read the organisation
proposedApproach: Boot Fastify with authModels('MEMBER', activeSubscription()) and organisation.findUnique returning a public org, register organisationRoutes, inject GET / with a MEMBER Bearer token; assert statusCode 200.

guarantee: Both GET / and PATCH / are blocked by subscriptionGuard with 403 {code:'NO_SUBSCRIPTION'} when the organisation has no subscription record, and the service/update is never reached.
gapDescription: subscriptionGuard's NO_SUBSCRIPTION is proven for the board-member plugin in route-guards.test.ts ('board-member routes require an active subscription'), but NOT for organisationRoutes; the organisation plugin's hook ordering is unverified.
proposedTitle: organisation routes require an active subscription
proposedApproach: Boot Fastify with authModels(OWNER, null) (subscription.findUnique -> null) plus an organisation.findUnique that throws if reached, register organisationRoutes, inject GET / with an OWNER Bearer token; assert statusCode 403 and json().code === 'NO_SUBSCRIPTION'.

guarantee: An expired-trial / past-due-grace-expired / inactive subscription blocks GET and PATCH with the corresponding 403 code (TRIAL_EXPIRED | PAST_DUE_GRACE_EXPIRED | SUBSCRIPTION_INACTIVE).
gapDescription: No test exercises subscriptionGuard's non-NO_SUBSCRIPTION rejection codes through the organisation plugin.
proposedTitle: organisation routes reject an expired trial
proposedApproach: Boot Fastify with authModels(OWNER, { status:'TRIALING', trialEndsAt: new Date(Date.now()-1000), currentPeriodEnd: null }) and a throwing organisation.findUnique, register organisationRoutes, inject GET /; assert 403 and json().code === 'TRIAL_EXPIRED'. (requireCompletePlan does NOT apply to this route group, so no COMPLETE-only sub-clause is needed.)

guarantee: PATCH / rejects malformed bodies with 400 {code:'VALIDATION_ERROR'} (e.g. name longer than 300 chars, contactEmail not an email, website not a URL, dateInputSchema rejecting non-ISO dates, legalForm/complexity/charitablePurpose outside their enums) and never calls organisation.update or leaks a 500/stack.
gapDescription: updateOrganisationSchema is parsed in the route handler and mapped to 400 VALIDATION_ERROR on ZodError, but no test asserts any malformed PATCH body is rejected with that code before the write; the route-level Zod handling for this plugin is entirely unproven.
proposedTitle: PATCH / rejects malformed bodies with VALIDATION_ERROR and skips the write
proposedApproach: Boot Fastify with authModels(OWNER, activeSubscription()) plus organisation.update flipping updateCalled, register organisationRoutes, inject PATCH / with an OWNER Bearer token and an invalid payload (e.g. { contactEmail: 'not-an-email' } and separately { financialYearEnd: '31-12-2026' }); for each assert statusCode 400, json().code === 'VALIDATION_ERROR', and updateCalled === false.

guarantee: Both endpoints reject unauthenticated requests (missing/invalid/revoked token) with 401 {code:'UNAUTHORIZED'} and reject authenticated-but-email-unverified users with 403 {code:'EMAIL_NOT_VERIFIED'} via authGuard, before any service call.
testTitle: authGuard rejects access tokens whose backing session has been revoked
gapDescription: authGuard's behaviour is proven directly as a unit (revoked session -> 401 UNAUTHORIZED, unverified -> 403 EMAIL_NOT_VERIFIED, and role/org resolved from the DB not the JWT), but no test confirms the organisation plugin actually wires authGuard as an onRequest hook ahead of the handler, so a regression removing the hook from organisationRoutes would not be caught.
proposedTitle: organisation routes require authentication
proposedApproach: Boot Fastify with authModels(OWNER, activeSubscription()), register organisationRoutes, inject GET / and PATCH / with NO authorization header; assert each returns statusCode 401 and json().code === 'UNAUTHORIZED'.

guarantee: PUT /records/:standardId with a MEMBER token returns 403 {code:'FORBIDDEN'} and the complianceRecord.upsert mock is NOT called.
gapDescription: requireAdmin is wired as a preHandler on PUT /records/:standardId, but no test registers complianceRoutes and injects a MEMBER request. route-guards.test.ts only proves requireAdmin for the board-member route plugin, not for compliance.
proposedTitle: a MEMBER cannot upsert a compliance record (requireAdmin)
proposedApproach: Boot Fastify({logger:false}); decorate prisma with authSession.findFirst->{id:'sess-1'}, user.findUnique->{role:'MEMBER',emailVerified:true,organisationId:'org-1'}, subscription.findUnique->ACTIVE, and a complianceRecord.upsert spy. Register complianceRoutes; inject PUT /records/s1 with a MEMBER Bearer token and body {reportingYear:2026,status:'COMPLIANT'}. Assert 403, json().code==='FORBIDDEN', and the upsert spy was never called.

guarantee: PUT /signoff with a MEMBER token returns 403 {code:'FORBIDDEN'} and the complianceSignoff.upsert mock is NOT called.
gapDescription: requireAdmin gates PUT /signoff but there is no HTTP-level test exercising the compliance plugin with a MEMBER token.
proposedTitle: a MEMBER cannot update the board sign-off (requireAdmin)
proposedApproach: Same harness as above with a complianceSignoff.upsert spy. Inject PUT /signoff with a MEMBER Bearer token and body {reportingYear:2026,status:'DRAFT'}. Assert 403, code==='FORBIDDEN', and the upsert spy was never called.

guarantee: An ADMIN (and OWNER) token is accepted by requireAdmin on both PUT /records/:standardId and PUT /signoff (reaches the service and returns 2xx).
gapDescription: The positive side of requireAdmin for the compliance plugin (ADMIN/OWNER allowed through) is only proven for board-member routes, not compliance.
proposedTitle: an ADMIN may upsert a compliance record and the board sign-off
proposedApproach: Boot the app with user.findUnique->role:'ADMIN'; mock organisation.findUniqueOrThrow, subscription.findUnique->{plan:'COMPLETE'} (ACTIVE), governanceStandard.findUnique->{id:'s1',isCore:true}, complianceRecord.upsert->{id} and complianceSignoff.upsert->a full row. Inject both PUTs with an ADMIN token and valid bodies; assert each returns 200.

guarantee: Every compliance endpoint with a missing/invalid Bearer token returns 401 {code:'UNAUTHORIZED'} and no service query runs.
gapDescription: authGuard is attached to the compliance plugin via onRequest, but authGuard's 401 behavior is only proven against board-member/team plugins (route-guards.test.ts) and as a unit (auth-isolation.test.ts). No test injects an unauthenticated request into complianceRoutes.
proposedTitle: compliance routes require authentication
proposedApproach: Register complianceRoutes with a prisma mock whose query spies throw if called. Inject GET /principles, GET /records?year=2026, PUT /records/s1, GET /signoff and PUT /signoff with no authorization header; assert each is 401 with code 'UNAUTHORIZED' and that no compliance prisma spy ran.

guarantee: When the organisation has no subscription row, every scope-resolving compliance read/write (getRecords/getRecord/getSummary/getPrinciplesForOrganisation/upsertRecord) throws AppError 403 {code:'NO_SUBSCRIPTION'} before any compliance table is touched.
testTitle: compliance scope requires an active subscription
gapDescription: The existing test proves NO_SUBSCRIPTION only for getRecords. getRecord, getSummary, getPrinciplesForOrganisation, getPrincipleForOrganisation and upsertRecord all route through the same getOrganisationComplianceScope, but their NO_SUBSCRIPTION behavior is not individually asserted (and upsertRecord must additionally prove the upsert mock is not called).
proposedTitle: compliance writes are refused (and not persisted) without a subscription
proposedApproach: Using buildService({plan:null}) with a complianceRecord.upsert spy, assert upsertRecord('org_1','s1','u1',{reportingYear:2026}) rejects with code 'NO_SUBSCRIPTION' and that the upsert spy was never called; add a parallel rejects() for getSummary and getRecord.

guarantee: An ESSENTIALS/SIMPLE org's compliance reads (records, summary, principles) are restricted to isCore standards; a COMPLETE/COMPLEX org receives additional standards.
testTitle: getRecords for an Essentials/Simple org restricts to core standards

guarantee: upsertRecord for an out-of-plan (non-core) standard on an ESSENTIALS org throws 403 {code:'COMPLIANCE_STANDARD_NOT_INCLUDED_IN_PLAN'} and does NOT write the record; an unknown standard throws 404 STANDARD_NOT_FOUND; a core standard or a COMPLETE/COMPLEX additional standard is allowed.
testTitle: upsertRecord blocks a non-core standard for an Essentials org (no billing bypass)

guarantee: getRecord for a non-core standard on an ESSENTIALS org throws 403 COMPLIANCE_STANDARD_NOT_INCLUDED_IN_PLAN (read path is gated identically to the write path via ensureStandardIncludedInPlan).
gapDescription: ensureStandardIncludedInPlan is exercised only through upsertRecord in tests; getRecord calls the same guard but no test asserts the read of an out-of-plan single standard is refused.
proposedTitle: getRecord refuses an out-of-plan standard for an Essentials org
proposedApproach: buildService({plan:'ESSENTIALS',complexity:'SIMPLE',standard:{id:'s_extra',isCore:false}}); assert.rejects(getRecord('org_1','s_extra',2026)) with code 'COMPLIANCE_STANDARD_NOT_INCLUDED_IN_PLAN', and assert complianceRecord.findUnique was not called.

guarantee: upsertRecord writes only to the requesting org: the complianceRecord.upsert where-key and create payload carry organisationId from request.user, so a record for standard s1 in org A can never be created/overwritten under org B.
testTitle: upsertRecord allows a non-core standard for a Complete/Complex org
gapDescription: Existing tests assert that upsert RUNS but never inspect the upsert args to confirm where.organisationId_standardId_reportingYear.organisationId and create.organisationId equal the passed organisationId. The org-scoping of the write is therefore not directly proven.
proposedTitle: upsertRecord scopes the composite key and create payload to the caller organisation
proposedApproach: buildService({plan:'COMPLETE',complexity:'COMPLEX',standard:{id:'s1',isCore:true}}); call upsertRecord('org_A','s1','u1',{reportingYear:2026,status:'COMPLIANT'}); read the captured complianceRecord.upsert args and assert where.organisationId_standardId_reportingYear.organisationId==='org_A' and args.create.organisationId==='org_A' and updatedById==='u1'.

guarantee: getRecord/getRecords/getSummary never return another org's compliance records: each prisma query embeds organisationId from request.user (composite key for getRecord, where.organisationId for getRecords/getSummary).
testTitle: getRecords for an Essentials/Simple org restricts to core standards
gapDescription: The existing getRecords test inspects where.standard but not where.organisationId. No test asserts that getRecord's composite where-key or getSummary's complianceRecord.findMany where carries the caller's organisationId.
proposedTitle: compliance reads are scoped to the caller organisation
proposedApproach: buildService({plan:'COMPLETE',complexity:'COMPLEX'}); call getRecords('org_A',2026) and getSummary('org_A',2026); assert the captured complianceRecord.findMany where.organisationId==='org_A'. For getRecord, capture complianceRecord.findUnique args and assert where.organisationId_standardId_reportingYear.organisationId==='org_A'.

guarantee: upsertSignoff and getSignoff operate only on the caller org's sign-off: the complianceSignoff composite key organisationId_reportingYear is built from request.user.organisationId, so org B can never read or overwrite org A's board approval.
gapDescription: No test inspects the complianceSignoff.upsert or findUnique where-key. getSignoff has only a no-row DRAFT-default test, and upsertSignoff has no test at all, so its org-scoping (and updatedById) of the write is unproven.
proposedTitle: upsertSignoff scopes the composite key to the caller organisation
proposedApproach: Extend buildService to capture complianceSignoff.upsert args; call upsertSignoff('org_A','u1',{reportingYear:2026,status:'DRAFT'}); assert where.organisationId_reportingYear.organisationId==='org_A', create.organisationId==='org_A', and updatedById==='u1'.

guarantee: PUT /records/:standardId rejects a malformed body (e.g. reportingYear out of [2018,2100], a non-enum status, or an over-5000-char text field) with 400 {code:'VALIDATION_ERROR'} and does NOT call complianceRecord.upsert.
gapDescription: upsertComplianceRecordSchema is parsed in the route and ZodError is mapped to 400 VALIDATION_ERROR, but no test injects an invalid body into the registered compliance plugin.
proposedTitle: PUT /records rejects an invalid body before writing
proposedApproach: Register complianceRoutes with an ADMIN token and a complianceRecord.upsert spy. Inject PUT /records/s1 with body {reportingYear:1999} (below min) and separately {reportingYear:2026,status:'BOGUS'}; assert 400 and json().code==='VALIDATION_ERROR' and the upsert spy never ran.

guarantee: PUT /signoff enforces the superRefine rule: status 'APPROVED' without boardMeetingDate/minuteReference/approvedByName returns 400 {code:'VALIDATION_ERROR'} and does NOT call complianceSignoff.upsert; an invalid status enum or a non-ISO boardMeetingDate is also 400.
gapDescription: The conditional-required superRefine and date-format refinement in upsertComplianceSignoffSchema are entirely untested; no route or service test exercises an APPROVED-without-required-fields body.
proposedTitle: PUT /signoff requires approval evidence when status is APPROVED
proposedApproach: ADMIN token, complianceSignoff.upsert spy. Inject PUT /signoff with body {reportingYear:2026,status:'APPROVED'} (omitting boardMeetingDate/minuteReference/approvedByName); assert 400, code 'VALIDATION_ERROR', and the upsert spy never ran. Add a second inject with status:'APPROVED' and a malformed boardMeetingDate:'not-a-date' expecting the same 400.

guarantee: GET endpoints that require ?year reject a missing or non-numeric/out-of-range year with 400 {code:'VALIDATION_ERROR'} (complianceQuerySchema), not a 500.
gapDescription: complianceQuerySchema.parse runs on GET /records, /records/:standardId, /summary, /signoff and maps ZodError to 400 VALIDATION_ERROR, but no test injects a missing/invalid year into the compliance plugin.
proposedTitle: compliance year-scoped reads reject a missing or invalid year
proposedApproach: Authenticated (any role) token, subscription ACTIVE. Inject GET /records (no year) and GET /summary?year=abcd; assert each returns 400 with code 'VALIDATION_ERROR'.

guarantee: GET /principles/:principleId returns 404 {code:'PRINCIPLE_NOT_FOUND'} for an unknown principle id (rather than 500 or leaking null).
gapDescription: The 404 PRINCIPLE_NOT_FOUND branch is handled in the route when the service returns null, but no test registers the plugin and injects an unknown principleId.
proposedTitle: GET /principles/:id returns 404 for an unknown principle
proposedApproach: Authenticated token, subscription ACTIVE, governancePrinciple.findUnique->null (plus organisation/subscription mocks for scope). Inject GET /principles/does-not-exist; assert 404 and json().code==='PRINCIPLE_NOT_FOUND'.

guarantee: subscriptionGuard blocks compliance routes for trial-expired / past-due-grace-expired / inactive subscriptions with the matching 403 code (TRIAL_EXPIRED | PAST_DUE_GRACE_EXPIRED | SUBSCRIPTION_INACTIVE | NO_SUBSCRIPTION).
gapDescription: subscriptionGuard is on the compliance onRequest chain, but its per-status codes are proven only against the board-member plugin (route-guards.test.ts proves NO_SUBSCRIPTION there) — not for compliance, and not for the expired/past-due/inactive variants on any plugin in this area.
proposedTitle: compliance routes are blocked when the subscription is not in good standing
proposedApproach: Register complianceRoutes; for each subscription mock {status:'TRIALING',trialEndsAt:past}, {status:'PAST_DUE',currentPeriodEnd:past}, {status:'CANCELED'} and null, inject GET /records?year=2026 with a valid token and assert 403 with codes TRIAL_EXPIRED, PAST_DUE_GRACE_EXPIRED, SUBSCRIPTION_INACTIVE and NO_SUBSCRIPTION respectively.

guarantee: Not applicable: the compliance route group calls no external provider (Stripe/Supabase/Resend) — it only reads/writes Prisma — so there is no provider-unconfigured 503 path to prove.
gapDescription: ComplianceService and the route handlers depend solely on app.prisma; no Stripe/Supabase/Resend client is constructed or invoked anywhere in routes/compliance/index.ts or services/compliance.service.ts, so the graceful-degradation concern does not apply to this area.

guarantee: Not applicable: compliance writes are upserts keyed on (organisationId, standardId, reportingYear) for records and (organisationId, reportingYear) for sign-off, which are naturally idempotent; there are no webhooks, reminder dedupe logs, or reconciliation retries in this route group.
gapDescription: The idempotency concern targets StripeWebhookEvent / DeadlineReminderLog / document-deletion reconciliation, none of which exist in the compliance area. The upsert-by-composite-key design means repeated PUTs are inherently safe, so there is no separate dedupe mechanism to prove.

guarantee: BoardMemberService.update(orgA, id-of-org-B-member, ...) throws AppError 404 BOARD_MEMBER_NOT_FOUND and never calls prisma.boardMember.update (no cross-org write).
testTitle: board member update rejects a member from another organisation

guarantee: BoardMemberService.remove(orgA, id-of-org-B-member) throws AppError 404 BOARD_MEMBER_NOT_FOUND and never calls prisma.boardMember.delete (no cross-org delete).
testTitle: board member remove rejects a member from another organisation

guarantee: BoardMemberService.create stamps the caller's organisationId onto the new record (a caller cannot create a board member attributed to another org).
testTitle: board member create scopes to the organisation

guarantee: BoardMemberService.list(orgA, ...) passes where {organisationId: orgA} to both findMany and count, so it can only return org A's board members and its total reflects only org A.
gapDescription: No test asserts the organisationId filter on the list/count queries; the existing boardPrisma mock returns [] for findMany and 0 for count without inspecting the where clause, so a regression dropping the org filter from list would pass unnoticed.
proposedTitle: board member list scopes findMany and count to the organisation
proposedApproach: Use the boardPrisma helper but capture the args pushed for boardMember.findMany and boardMember.count; call service.list('org_1', 1, 50) and assert both recorded where objects equal {organisationId:'org_1'}. No write mocks are involved (list is read-only).

guarantee: A MEMBER token is rejected with 403 {code:'FORBIDDEN'} on POST /, PATCH /:id and DELETE /:id (requireAdmin blocks writes), while an ADMIN is allowed (POST / returns 201).
testTitle: a MEMBER cannot create, update, or delete board members (requireAdmin)

guarantee: GET / (list) is readable by a non-admin MEMBER (read is intentionally not requireAdmin-gated) and returns 200.
testTitle: a MEMBER can still read board members, and an ADMIN can create them

guarantee: Every board-member endpoint rejects an unauthenticated request with 401 {code:'UNAUTHORIZED'} (authGuard onRequest hook applies to the whole group).
testTitle: board-member routes require authentication
gapDescription: Only GET / is asserted to require auth. POST /, PATCH /:id and DELETE /:id are not individually checked for the 401-without-token path; since authGuard is a group-level onRequest hook this is low risk, but the write endpoints' unauthenticated rejection is not directly proven.
proposedTitle: board-member write routes reject unauthenticated requests
proposedApproach: Build the board app with buildBoardApp('ADMIN') and inject POST / (with a valid payload), PATCH /bm-1 and DELETE /bm-1 with NO authorization header; assert each returns 401 and json().code === 'UNAUTHORIZED'. The create/update/delete prisma mocks must not be relied on (handler is never reached).

guarantee: Board-member endpoints require an active subscription: subscriptionGuard rejects a missing subscription with 403 {code:'NO_SUBSCRIPTION'}.
testTitle: board-member routes require an active subscription
gapDescription: Only the subscription===null -> NO_SUBSCRIPTION case is proven on this route. The other subscriptionGuard denial codes (TRIAL_EXPIRED, PAST_DUE_GRACE_EXPIRED, SUBSCRIPTION_INACTIVE) are not exercised through the board-members group. requireCompletePlan does not apply (board-members has no requireCompletePlan), so COMPLETE-only gating is correctly N/A.
proposedTitle: board-member routes reject an expired trial and inactive subscription
proposedApproach: Call buildBoardApp('ADMIN', {status:'TRIALING', trialEndsAt: new Date(Date.now()-1000), currentPeriodEnd:null}) and inject GET / with a valid ADMIN token; assert 403 and json().code === 'TRIAL_EXPIRED'. Repeat with {status:'CANCELED',...} asserting code SUBSCRIPTION_INACTIVE. No board-member write mock should be invoked.

guarantee: POST / with a malformed body (e.g. missing required name/role/appointedDate, or appointedDate not an ISO date) is rejected with 400 {code:'VALIDATION_ERROR'} and never calls prisma.boardMember.create (no partial record written, no 500/stack leak).
gapDescription: No test exercises the Zod validation branch on POST /. The requireAdmin/ADMIN create test sends a well-formed body, so the 400 VALIDATION_ERROR path and the guarantee that create is not called on invalid input are unproven.
proposedTitle: POST /board-members rejects malformed bodies with VALIDATION_ERROR and does not create
proposedApproach: Build a Fastify app as in route-guards with an ADMIN token and a boardMember.create mock that flips a createCalled flag; inject POST / with payload {name:'', role:'Chair', appointedDate:'not-a-date'}; assert statusCode 400, json().code === 'VALIDATION_ERROR', and createCalled === false.

guarantee: PATCH /:id with a malformed body (e.g. email not an email, or appointedDate not ISO) is rejected with 400 {code:'VALIDATION_ERROR'} and never calls prisma.boardMember.update or findFirst (no foreign lookup or write on invalid input).
gapDescription: No test exercises the Zod branch on PATCH /:id. The update service-level isolation tests bypass the route schema by calling the service directly, so the route's 400 VALIDATION_ERROR mapping and the no-write guarantee on invalid input are unproven.
proposedTitle: PATCH /board-members/:id rejects an invalid email with VALIDATION_ERROR and does not update
proposedApproach: Build the board app with an ADMIN token; set boardMember.update and findFirst mocks to flip called flags; inject PATCH /bm-1 with payload {email:'not-an-email'}; assert statusCode 400, json().code === 'VALIDATION_ERROR', and that neither update nor findFirst was called (validation happens before the service).

guarantee: Board-member endpoints do not integrate with Stripe/Supabase/Resend (the service is constructed with only prisma), so there is no external-dependency 503 path to prove for this route group.
gapDescription: Non-obvious because sibling route groups (team, billing, documents) take email/storage/Stripe clients; board-members deliberately does not, so the graceful-degradation concern has no applicable surface here.

guarantee: GET /:id for a document owned by org B returns 404 {code:'DOCUMENT_NOT_FOUND'} for an org-A user, because DocumentService.getById scopes findFirst by {id, organisationId} and throws AppError 404 when null.
gapDescription: No test injects a foreign-org document id against GET /:id; service scoping is implemented (document.service.ts getById) but unproven at HTTP level for documents.
proposedTitle: GET /:id returns 404 for a document belonging to another organisation
proposedApproach: Build the app with document.findFirst resolving null (simulating the org-scoped where excluding the foreign row) and organisation.findUniqueOrThrow returning {complexity:'SIMPLE'}. Inject GET /foreign-doc with the org-1 ADMIN authHeader; assert statusCode 404 and json().code === 'DOCUMENT_NOT_FOUND'.

guarantee: GET /:id/download for a document owned by org B returns 404 {code:'DOCUMENT_NOT_FOUND'} for an org-A user (service.getStoragePath scopes findFirst by {id, organisationId}), and never calls StorageService.getSignedUrl.
gapDescription: No test exercises GET /:id/download with a foreign-org id; getStoragePath's org scoping and the 'getSignedUrl not called' assertion are unproven.
proposedTitle: GET /:id/download returns 404 and never signs a URL for a foreign-org document
proposedApproach: Stub StorageService.prototype.getSignedUrl to set signedCalled=true. Build app with document.findFirst (select fileUrl) resolving null. Inject GET /foreign-doc/download with org-1 authHeader; assert 404 / code DOCUMENT_NOT_FOUND and signedCalled === false.

guarantee: StorageService.getSignedUrl rejects a storagePath outside the caller's organisation prefix (org-b/..., ../org-a/..., bare org id) with AppError 403 {code:'STORAGE_PATH_FORBIDDEN'} before any Supabase access, so a tampered fileUrl cannot sign another org's object.
testTitle: getSignedUrl rejects storage paths outside the organisation prefix before storage access

guarantee: StorageService.deleteFile rejects a storagePath outside the caller's organisation prefix with AppError 403 {code:'STORAGE_PATH_FORBIDDEN'} before any Supabase access, so the delete path cannot remove another org's object.
testTitle: deleteFile rejects storage paths outside the organisation prefix before storage access

guarantee: GET /_local-download enforces the organisation prefix on the supplied ?path via StorageService.readLocalFile -> assertOrganisationStoragePath, returning 403 {code:'STORAGE_PATH_FORBIDDEN'} when an org-A user requests an org-B path, preventing cross-org local-file reads.
testTitle: local storage driver writes, reads, signs, and deletes files without Supabase
gapDescription: The local-driver happy path proves readLocalFile reads the caller's own org file, but no test asserts readLocalFile (or the GET /_local-download route) rejects a foreign-org ?path with 403 STORAGE_PATH_FORBIDDEN; the assertOrganisationStoragePath guard inside readLocalFile is only covered for getSignedUrl/deleteFile, not the read path nor the route.
proposedTitle: readLocalFile rejects storage paths outside the organisation prefix before reading
proposedApproach: With DOCUMENT_STORAGE_DRIVER='local', call new StorageService().readLocalFile('org-a','org-b/policy.pdf') and assert it rejects with AppError 403 / code STORAGE_PATH_FORBIDDEN (reuse assertForbiddenStoragePath), including the '../org-a/policy.pdf' and bare 'org-a' cases.

guarantee: POST /:id/link-standard (and alias /:id/standards) for a document owned by org B returns 404 {code:'DOCUMENT_NOT_FOUND'} for an org-A user and never calls documentStandardLink.create, because linkStandard scopes the document findFirst by {id, organisationId}.
gapDescription: The Essentials plan-gating test proves linkStandard's plan branch but uses an owned document (findFirst returns the row). No test injects a foreign-org document id to prove the 404 + 'create not called' isolation branch.
proposedTitle: POST /:id/standards rejects linking when the document belongs to another organisation
proposedApproach: Build app with document.findFirst resolving null and documentStandardLink.create setting linkCreated=true. Inject POST /foreign-doc/standards with payload {standardId:'s1'} and org-1 authHeader; assert 404 / code DOCUMENT_NOT_FOUND and linkCreated === false.

guarantee: DELETE /:id/unlink-standard (and alias /:id/standards/:standardId) for a document owned by org B returns 404 {code:'DOCUMENT_NOT_FOUND'} for an org-A user and never calls documentStandardLink.deleteMany, because unlinkStandard scopes the document findFirst by {id, organisationId}.
gapDescription: No test covers unlinkStandard at all; the foreign-org 404 branch and the 'deleteMany not called' guarantee are unproven.
proposedTitle: DELETE /:id/standards/:standardId rejects unlinking when the document belongs to another organisation
proposedApproach: Build app with document.findFirst resolving null and documentStandardLink.deleteMany setting deleteCalled=true. Inject DELETE /foreign-doc/standards/s1 with org-1 authHeader; assert 404 / code DOCUMENT_NOT_FOUND and deleteCalled === false.

guarantee: DELETE /:id for a document owned by org B returns 404 {code:'DOCUMENT_NOT_FOUND'} for an org-A user, never creates a DocumentStorageDeletion outbox row, never deletes the DB row, and never calls StorageService.deleteFile (remove() scopes the findFirst by {id, organisationId}).
gapDescription: The delete tests use an owned document (findFirst returns the row). No test injects a foreign-org id to prove remove() throws 404 and performs none of the write/outbox/storage side effects.
proposedTitle: DELETE /:id rejects and performs no side effects for a document belonging to another organisation
proposedApproach: Stub StorageService.prototype.deleteFile to set storageCalled=true. Build app with document.findFirst resolving null, document.delete and documentStorageDeletion.create throwing if invoked. Inject DELETE /foreign-doc with org-1 authHeader; assert 404 / code DOCUMENT_NOT_FOUND and storageCalled === false.

guarantee: Uploaded objects are namespaced to the caller's organisation: StorageService.uploadFile writes to `${organisationId}/<timestamp>-<sanitised>` so one org's upload can never overwrite or land under another org's prefix.
testTitle: local storage driver writes, reads, signs, and deletes files without Supabase
gapDescription: The local-driver test asserts the path matches /^org-local\/\d+-.../ for the caller's own org, proving the prefix shape, but nothing asserts the prefix uses the request.user.organisationId end-to-end through POST / (i.e. that an authenticated org-1 upload cannot be stored under another org's prefix); this is implicit, not directly falsified.
proposedTitle: document upload stores the object under the caller's organisation prefix
proposedApproach: Stub StorageService.prototype.uploadFile to capture (organisationId, filename). Inject a valid PDF upload via POST / with the org-1 ADMIN authHeader and a passing create mock; assert the captured organisationId === 'org-1' so the storage prefix is derived from request.user, not client input.

guarantee: POST / (upload) rejects a MEMBER with 403 {code:'FORBIDDEN'} via requireAdmin and never calls StorageService.uploadFile or document.create.
gapDescription: requireAdmin is wired as a preHandler on POST /, but the existing upload tests all use an ADMIN token; no documents test proves a MEMBER is rejected before any storage/db write. (route-guards.test.ts proves requireAdmin only for the board-members route group.)
proposedTitle: a MEMBER cannot upload a document (requireAdmin)
proposedApproach: Sign a MEMBER token (role:'MEMBER') and set the user mock role to 'MEMBER'. Stub uploadFile to set uploadCalled=true. Inject a valid multipart PDF to POST /; assert 403 / code FORBIDDEN and uploadCalled === false.

guarantee: DELETE /:id rejects a MEMBER with 403 {code:'FORBIDDEN'} via requireAdmin and never calls service.remove or StorageService.deleteFile.
gapDescription: requireAdmin is wired on DELETE /:id but no documents test exercises a MEMBER token against it.
proposedTitle: a MEMBER cannot delete a document (requireAdmin)
proposedApproach: Sign a MEMBER token and set user role 'MEMBER'. Stub StorageService.prototype.deleteFile to set storageCalled=true and make document.delete throw if called. Inject DELETE /doc-1; assert 403 / code FORBIDDEN and storageCalled === false.

guarantee: POST /:id/link-standard, POST /:id/standards, DELETE /:id/unlink-standard, and DELETE /:id/standards/:standardId each reject a MEMBER with 403 {code:'FORBIDDEN'} via requireAdmin and never call documentStandardLink.create/deleteMany.
gapDescription: All four standard-link write endpoints carry requireAdmin, but the only existing standards test ('Essentials organisations cannot link...') uses an ADMIN token and asserts a plan error, not the MEMBER/role boundary.
proposedTitle: a MEMBER cannot link or unlink a document to a governance standard (requireAdmin)
proposedApproach: Sign a MEMBER token, set user role 'MEMBER', make documentStandardLink.create/deleteMany set a called flag. Inject POST /doc-1/standards and DELETE /doc-1/standards/s1; assert each is 403 / code FORBIDDEN and neither write mock was called.

guarantee: An ESSENTIALS (or COMPLEX-but-not-COMPLETE / SIMPLE) organisation cannot link a document to a non-core ('additional') governance standard: linkStandard throws AppError 403 {code:'COMPLIANCE_STANDARD_NOT_INCLUDED_IN_PLAN'} and never calls documentStandardLink.create.
testTitle: Essentials organisations cannot link documents to additional governance standards

guarantee: Uploads are gated by a per-plan storage quota (ESSENTIALS 2 GiB, COMPLETE 10 GiB): when used+requested bytes exceed the plan quota, create throws AppError 403 {code:'DOCUMENT_STORAGE_QUOTA_EXCEEDED'} before document.create runs, and the already-uploaded object is cleaned up.
testTitle: document upload rejects files that would exceed the plan storage quota and cleans up storage

guarantee: Upload with no subscription record is blocked: assertStorageQuota throws AppError 403 {code:'NO_SUBSCRIPTION'} before document.create, so an org without a subscription cannot store documents.
gapDescription: subscription.findUnique returning null in assertStorageQuota (document.service.ts) throws NO_SUBSCRIPTION, but no test drives the upload path with a missing subscription at the quota layer (the subscriptionGuard hook also returns NO_SUBSCRIPTION earlier, but that guard path is not tested for documents either).
proposedTitle: document upload is blocked when the organisation has no subscription record
proposedApproach: Build app where subscription.findUnique resolves null (and bypass/relax subscriptionGuard so the request reaches create, or assert the guard's 403 NO_SUBSCRIPTION directly). Stub uploadFile, make document.create set createCalled=true. Inject a valid PDF upload; assert 403 / code NO_SUBSCRIPTION and createCalled === false.

guarantee: subscriptionGuard blocks every documents endpoint when the subscription is expired/inactive: a TRIAL_EXPIRED / PAST_DUE_GRACE_EXPIRED / SUBSCRIPTION_INACTIVE org receives 403 with the corresponding code on GET / and POST /, regardless of role.
gapDescription: subscriptionGuard is registered as an onRequest hook on the documents plugin, but no documents test exercises an expired/inactive subscription; existing tests always use an active/trialing subscription mock.
proposedTitle: documents endpoints are blocked when the subscription trial has expired
proposedApproach: Build app with subscription.findUnique resolving {status:'TRIALING', trialEndsAt: new Date(Date.now()-60000), plan:'ESSENTIALS'}. Inject GET / with the ADMIN authHeader; assert 403 and json().code === 'TRIAL_EXPIRED'.

guarantee: Upload rejects a file whose declared MIME type is allowed but whose magic-byte signature does not match (e.g. a .pdf that is not %PDF-), returning 400 {code:'INVALID_FILE_SIGNATURE'} before StorageService.uploadFile is called.
testTitle: document upload rejects files whose signature does not match the claimed document type

guarantee: Upload rejects a disallowed MIME type (e.g. application/msword legacy macro-capable Office) with 400 {code:'INVALID_MIME_TYPE'} before StorageService.uploadFile is called.
testTitle: document upload rejects macro-capable legacy Office files before storage

guarantee: Upload of a file exceeding the 10 MB limit returns 413 {code:'FILE_TOO_LARGE'} (translating the Fastify FST_REQ_FILE_TOO_LARGE / multipart fileSize error) without storing the file.
testTitle: document upload translates multipart file size errors to 413

guarantee: Upload with too many multipart fields, more than one file, or an oversized field value returns 413 {code:'MULTIPART_LIMIT_EXCEEDED'} before StorageService.uploadFile is called.
testTitle: document upload rejects requests with too many multipart fields before storage

guarantee: Upload with malformed metadata fields (missing required name, invalid category enum, oversized name/description/owner, malformed approvedDate/nextReviewDate) is rejected by uploadDocumentSchema with 400 {code:'VALIDATION_ERROR'} and a details array, never a 500 or stack leak.
gapDescription: The route catches ZodError and returns 400 VALIDATION_ERROR, but no test drives uploadDocumentSchema.parse failures (e.g. invalid category, missing name, bad date) through POST /; only signature/MIME/multipart and quota validations are tested.
proposedTitle: document upload rejects malformed metadata with VALIDATION_ERROR before storage
proposedApproach: Stub uploadFile to set uploadCalled=true. Inject a valid PDF body but with category:'NOT_A_CATEGORY' (and/or omit name); assert 400, json().code === 'VALIDATION_ERROR', json().details is an array, and uploadCalled === false.

guarantee: POST /:id/link-standard (and aliases) reject a missing/empty standardId via linkStandardSchema with 400 {code:'VALIDATION_ERROR'} before any documentStandardLink write.
gapDescription: linkStandardSchema.parse guards standardId on the link/unlink-standard endpoints, but no test sends an empty/missing standardId to assert the 400 VALIDATION_ERROR and that no link is created.
proposedTitle: POST /:id/standards rejects a missing standardId with VALIDATION_ERROR
proposedApproach: Make documentStandardLink.create set linkCreated=true. Inject POST /doc-1/standards with payload {} (or {standardId:''}) and ADMIN authHeader; assert 400 / code VALIDATION_ERROR and linkCreated === false.

guarantee: GET /_local-download without a ?path query returns 400 {code:'LOCAL_STORAGE_PATH_REQUIRED'} rather than a 500.
gapDescription: The route returns 400 LOCAL_STORAGE_PATH_REQUIRED when ?path is missing, but no test exercises this branch.
proposedTitle: GET /_local-download requires a path query parameter
proposedApproach: Inject GET /_local-download (no query) with the ADMIN authHeader and an active subscription mock; assert 400 and json().code === 'LOCAL_STORAGE_PATH_REQUIRED'.

guarantee: When the database create fails after a successful storage upload, the route deletes the just-stored object (StorageService.deleteFile called with the uploaded org/path) and surfaces the original error (500), preventing orphaned storage objects.
testTitle: document upload deletes the stored object when database creation fails

guarantee: When the database create fails AND the compensating storage cleanup also fails, the route preserves and returns the original database error (e.g. 400 DOCUMENT_CREATE_FAILED) rather than masking it with the cleanup error.
testTitle: document upload preserves the database error when storage cleanup fails

guarantee: When Supabase storage is unconfigured (no SUPABASE_URL / SERVICE_ROLE_KEY and not the local driver), the download path (GET /:id/download -> StorageService.getSignedUrl -> getSupabaseClient) returns a clean 503 {code:'STORAGE_NOT_CONFIGURED'} rather than a 500/stack leak.
gapDescription: getSupabaseClient throws AppError 503 STORAGE_NOT_CONFIGURED when secrets are absent, but no test asserts that an unconfigured-Supabase download (or upload) returns a clean 503 through the route; only the local-driver happy path and the path-prefix guard are tested.
proposedTitle: GET /:id/download returns 503 STORAGE_NOT_CONFIGURED when Supabase is unconfigured
proposedApproach: Ensure DOCUMENT_STORAGE_DRIVER is unset and delete SUPABASE_URL/SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY for the test. Build app with document.findFirst (select fileUrl) returning {fileUrl:'org-1/policy.pdf'}. Inject GET /doc-1/download with ADMIN authHeader; assert 503 and json().code === 'STORAGE_NOT_CONFIGURED'. Restore env in finally.

guarantee: GET /_local-download returns 503 {code:'STORAGE_NOT_CONFIGURED'} when the local storage driver is not enabled (readLocalFile guards isLocalStorageDriver), so the endpoint degrades cleanly instead of attempting a filesystem read.
gapDescription: readLocalFile throws 503 STORAGE_NOT_CONFIGURED when DOCUMENT_STORAGE_DRIVER!=='local', but no test exercises GET /_local-download in non-local mode.
proposedTitle: GET /_local-download returns 503 when the local storage driver is disabled
proposedApproach: With DOCUMENT_STORAGE_DRIVER unset, inject GET /_local-download?path=org-1/policy.pdf with ADMIN authHeader and active subscription; assert 503 and json().code === 'STORAGE_NOT_CONFIGURED'.

guarantee: Document deletion is crash-safe and reconciles exactly once: remove() creates a DocumentStorageDeletion outbox row and deletes the DB row in one transaction (outbox before storage), then deletes storage and marks the outbox row processed; ordering is outbox-create -> db-delete -> storage-delete -> mark-processed.
testTitle: document delete removes storage after deleting the database record

guarantee: If the DB delete fails inside remove()'s transaction, no storage object is deleted and the outbox row is not processed (the whole delete rolls back), so storage is never removed without a committed DB delete.
testTitle: document delete does not remove storage when database deletion fails

guarantee: If post-commit storage cleanup fails, the request still succeeds (204) and the failure is recorded on the outbox row (attempts incremented, sanitized lastError, claimedAt cleared) so the retry job can re-process it safely.
testTitle: document delete reports success when post-delete storage cleanup fails

guarantee: retryPendingStorageDeletions reconciles pending outbox rows exactly once: it claims unprocessed rows (via FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED when Postgres is available, else findMany of processedAt:null), deletes storage, marks processed on success, and on failure increments attempts + records a sanitized lastError + clears claimedAt (leaving the row pending for a safe retry).
testTitle: retryPendingStorageDeletions processes pending cleanup records

guarantee: A document-storage-cleanup job failure (the retry call throwing) fires the error-alert webhook with method JOB, url /jobs/document-storage-cleanup, code DOCUMENT_STORAGE_CLEANUP_FAILED, and no raw secrets/emails/paths in the payload or log.
testTitle: runDocumentStorageCleanup sends a sanitized operational alert when storage cleanup fails

guarantee: When the cleanup run completes without throwing but some storage deletions failed (result.failed > 0), the job still fires the error-alert webhook (errorName DocumentStorageCleanupFailure) so silent partial failures are surfaced.
testTitle: runDocumentStorageCleanup alerts when cleanup records failed storage deletions without throwing

guarantee: The readiness endpoint reflects real document-storage health: storageConfigured (StorageService.isConfigured) and storageBucketReachable (verifyBucket, with a readiness timeout) gate readiness, and a public/unreachable bucket flips readiness to 503 not_ready.
testTitle: verifyBucket returns false when the configured document bucket is public
gapDescription: verifyBucket's false-on-public-bucket result and the readiness timeout primitive are unit-tested, but no readiness-route test asserts that a false storageBucketReachable (or storageConfigured) actually drives the /readiness response to 503 status 'not_ready' with checks.storageBucketReachable === false; health-readiness.test.ts only covers the database check and auth gating.
proposedTitle: readiness reports not_ready when the document storage bucket is unreachable
proposedApproach: With READINESS_API_KEY set and a healthy $queryRaw mock, stub StorageService.prototype.verifyBucket to resolve false (and isConfigured true). Inject GET /api/v1/health/readiness with the readiness key header; assert statusCode 503, body.status === 'not_ready', and body.checks.storageBucketReachable === false.

guarantee: DeadlineService.update(orgA, idOwnedByOrgB, data) performs findFirst({where:{id, organisationId:orgA}}), gets null, throws AppError 404 DEADLINE_NOT_FOUND, and never calls prisma.deadline.update.
testTitle: update rejects a deadline from another organisation (no cross-tenant write)

guarantee: DeadlineService.remove(orgA, idOwnedByOrgB) performs findFirst({where:{id, organisationId:orgA}}), gets null, throws AppError 404 DEADLINE_NOT_FOUND, and never calls prisma.deadline.delete.
testTitle: remove rejects a deadline from another organisation (no cross-tenant delete)

guarantee: DeadlineService.list(orgId, ...) issues findMany and count whose where clause is exactly {organisationId: orgId}, so a caller can only ever read deadlines of their own organisation.
testTitle: list reports hasMore based on total versus the returned page

guarantee: DeadlineService.create(orgId, data) sets data.organisationId to the caller's orgId (a member cannot create a deadline owned by another organisation).
testTitle: create scopes to the organisation and defaults reminderDays

guarantee: End-to-end through the HTTP stack: PATCH /:id and DELETE /:id for a deadline owned by org B, called with a valid org-A admin Bearer token, return 404 {code:'DEADLINE_NOT_FOUND'} and never invoke prisma.deadline.update / prisma.deadline.delete.
testTitle: update rejects a deadline from another organisation (no cross-tenant write)
gapDescription: Isolation is proven only by calling the service directly. No test registers deadlineRoutes and exercises the full authGuard+subscriptionGuard+requireAdmin+handler path, so it is unproven that the route wires request.user.organisationId into the service correctly and that handleError maps AppError 404 to a 404 response rather than a 500.
proposedTitle: PATCH/DELETE /:id for a deadline owned by another organisation returns 404 DEADLINE_NOT_FOUND without writing
proposedApproach: Boot Fastify({logger:false}); decorate prisma with authSession.findFirst (sess found), user.findUnique returning {organisationId:'org-1',role:'ADMIN',emailVerified:true}, subscription.findUnique returning an ACTIVE sub, and a deadline mock whose findFirst returns null with update/delete spies; register deadlineRoutes; inject PATCH /d-other (payload {title:'x'}) and DELETE /d-other with an ADMIN token for org-1; assert 404 and json().code==='DEADLINE_NOT_FOUND' and that the update/delete spies were NOT called.

guarantee: A MEMBER (valid token, active subscription) is rejected with 403 {code:'FORBIDDEN'} on POST /, PATCH /:id and DELETE /:id (requireAdmin), and the create/update/delete prisma write mocks are never called.
gapDescription: requireAdmin is wired onto all three deadline write routes but no test registers deadlineRoutes to prove a MEMBER is blocked. The analogous proof exists only for board-member routes (route-guards.test.ts), not for deadlines.
proposedTitle: a MEMBER cannot create, update, or delete deadlines (requireAdmin)
proposedApproach: Boot Fastify; decorate prisma with authModels(role:'MEMBER', activeSubscription) plus a deadline mock whose create/update/delete are spies; register deadlineRoutes; inject POST / (payload {title:'x',dueDate:'2026-09-30'}), PATCH /d1 (payload {title:'y'}), DELETE /d1 each with a MEMBER token; assert every response is 403 with json().code==='FORBIDDEN' and that none of the deadline write spies were called.

guarantee: An ADMIN/OWNER (valid token, active subscription) is allowed through requireAdmin: POST / returns 201, PATCH /:id returns 200, DELETE /:id returns 204.
gapDescription: The positive admin path through requireAdmin for deadline writes is unproven at the HTTP level (the service-level create/update tests bypass the guard entirely).
proposedTitle: an ADMIN may create, update, and delete deadlines
proposedApproach: Boot Fastify; decorate prisma with authModels(role:'ADMIN', activeSubscription) plus a deadline mock where findFirst returns {id,organisationId:'org-1'} and create/update/delete resolve; register deadlineRoutes; inject POST / (valid body) expecting 201, PATCH /d1 (valid body) expecting 200, DELETE /d1 expecting 204.

guarantee: GET / (list) is readable by a MEMBER (it carries authGuard+subscriptionGuard but NOT requireAdmin), returning 200.
gapDescription: No test proves that list is not over-gated (i.e. that a MEMBER can read). Proven for board-members but not deadlines.
proposedTitle: a MEMBER can read the deadline list
proposedApproach: Boot Fastify; decorate prisma with authModels(role:'MEMBER', activeSubscription) plus deadline.findMany=>[] and deadline.count=>0; register deadlineRoutes; inject GET / with a MEMBER token; assert statusCode===200.

guarantee: Every deadline route (GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE) rejects a request with no/invalid Bearer token, or a revoked session, with 401 {code:'UNAUTHORIZED'} before the handler runs.
gapDescription: authGuard is registered as an onRequest hook on the deadline plugin but no test registers deadlineRoutes to confirm unauthenticated requests are rejected. Equivalent proof exists for board-member and team routes only.
proposedTitle: deadline routes require authentication
proposedApproach: Boot Fastify; decorate prisma with a deadline mock plus subscription/user/authSession models; register deadlineRoutes; inject GET / with no authorization header (and a second case with a token whose authSession.findFirst returns null to simulate a revoked session); assert statusCode===401 and json().code==='UNAUTHORIZED'.

guarantee: A valid token whose owning user has emailVerified:false is rejected with 403 {code:'EMAIL_NOT_VERIFIED'} on deadline routes.
gapDescription: authGuard's email-verification branch is never exercised against the deadline route group.
proposedTitle: deadline routes reject a user whose email is not verified
proposedApproach: Boot Fastify; decorate prisma where user.findUnique returns {organisationId:'org-1',role:'ADMIN',emailVerified:false} and authSession.findFirst returns a session; register deadlineRoutes; inject GET / with a valid token; assert statusCode===403 and json().code==='EMAIL_NOT_VERIFIED'.

guarantee: All deadline routes are blocked by subscriptionGuard when the organisation has no subscription (403 NO_SUBSCRIPTION), an expired trial (403 TRIAL_EXPIRED), a past-due grace-expired sub (403 PAST_DUE_GRACE_EXPIRED), or an inactive sub (403 SUBSCRIPTION_INACTIVE), before the handler runs.
gapDescription: subscriptionGuard is registered as an onRequest hook on the deadline plugin but no test registers deadlineRoutes to prove the four subscription failure codes. The pattern is proven only for board-member routes (route-guards.test.ts proves NO_SUBSCRIPTION there).
proposedTitle: deadline routes require an active subscription
proposedApproach: Boot Fastify; decorate prisma with a valid auth user/session and subscription.findUnique returning, across cases, null / {status:'TRIALING',trialEndsAt:past} / {status:'PAST_DUE'} / {status:'CANCELLED'}; register deadlineRoutes; inject GET / with a valid token; assert statusCode===403 and json().code equals NO_SUBSCRIPTION / TRIAL_EXPIRED / PAST_DUE_GRACE_EXPIRED / SUBSCRIPTION_INACTIVE respectively, and that no deadline read/write mock ran.

guarantee: COMPLETE-only (requireCompletePlan) gating does not apply to the deadlines route group.
gapDescription: routes/deadlines/index.ts wires only authGuard, subscriptionGuard and requireAdmin; it never imports or applies requireCompletePlan, so there is no COMPLETE-plan feature boundary to prove for this group (deadlines are available on every active plan).

guarantee: POST / rejects a malformed/oversized create body (missing title, title>300 chars, missing/invalid dueDate, description>1000 chars, reminderDays element out of 1..365 or non-integer) with 400 {code:'VALIDATION_ERROR'} and never calls prisma.deadline.create.
gapDescription: createDeadlineSchema is parsed in-route and ZodError mapped to 400 VALIDATION_ERROR, but no test exercises POST / with invalid input to prove the 400 (vs a 500) and that the create write mock is not reached.
proposedTitle: POST / rejects malformed create bodies with 400 VALIDATION_ERROR and does not write
proposedApproach: Boot Fastify with an ADMIN/active-subscription auth context and a deadline.create spy; register deadlineRoutes; inject POST / with bodies {} (no title/dueDate), {title:'',dueDate:'2026-09-30'}, {title:'x',dueDate:'not-a-date'}, {title:'x',dueDate:'2026-09-30',reminderDays:[0]}; assert each is 400 with json().code==='VALIDATION_ERROR' and the create spy was never called.

guarantee: PATCH /:id rejects a malformed update body (e.g. title>300, invalid dueDate string, isComplete non-boolean, reminderDays out of range) with 400 {code:'VALIDATION_ERROR'} and never calls prisma.deadline.update.
gapDescription: updateDeadlineSchema parsing and its ZodError->400 mapping in the PATCH handler are never exercised by a test, so the safe-4xx (vs 500/stack-leak) behavior on bad update input is unproven, as is the guarantee that no update write occurs.
proposedTitle: PATCH /:id rejects malformed update bodies with 400 VALIDATION_ERROR and does not write
proposedApproach: Boot Fastify with an ADMIN/active-subscription auth context, deadline.findFirst=>{id,organisationId:'org-1'} and a deadline.update spy; register deadlineRoutes; inject PATCH /d1 with bodies {dueDate:'nope'}, {isComplete:'yes'}, {reminderDays:[400]}; assert each is 400 with json().code==='VALIDATION_ERROR' and the update spy was never called.

guarantee: A Stripe webhook event whose id already exists in StripeWebhookEvent is ignored before any Stripe retrieve or subscription mutation: handleWebhook returns early and neither stripe.subscriptions.retrieve, stripeWebhookEvent.create, nor subscription.upsert is called.
testTitle: duplicate Stripe webhook event ids are ignored before subscription mutation

guarantee: When two concurrent deliveries of the same event race past the pre-check, the in-transaction stripeWebhookEvent.create P2002 unique violation causes the second to return without mutating the subscription, while a P2002 originating from a different write (e.g. subscription.upsert on stripeSubscriptionId) is rethrown rather than swallowed.
testTitle: Stripe webhook unique errors outside the event ledger are rethrown

guarantee: The webhook ledger row is created inside the same $transaction as the subscription mutation (the create-then-mutate is atomic), so a failed subscription write does not leave a ledger row that would suppress a legitimate retry.
testTitle: Stripe webhook unique errors outside the event ledger are rethrown
gapDescription: The rethrow test proves the error propagates, but no test asserts the ledger.create and the subscription mutation share one $transaction such that the ledger row is rolled back when the mutation throws (i.e. that a retry of the same event id is NOT permanently suppressed after a failed write).
proposedTitle: a failed subscription write rolls back the webhook ledger row so the event can be retried
proposedApproach: Use billingHarness with a $transaction mock that records ledger.create then throws from subscription.upsert; after assert.rejects, assert the ledger row was created inside the same transaction callback (calls order shows stripeWebhookEvent.create between transaction.start and the throw) and that no committed ledger persists, proving create and mutate are co-transactional.

guarantee: POST /billing/webhooks with an invalid stripe-signature header returns 400 {code:'INVALID_STRIPE_SIGNATURE'} and never reaches handleWebhook (no subscription write).
testTitle: invalid Stripe webhook signatures return a client error

guarantee: POST /billing/webhooks with no stripe-signature header returns 400 {code:'MISSING_STRIPE_SIGNATURE'} and never reaches handleWebhook.
testTitle: missing Stripe webhook signatures return a client error

guarantee: createCheckoutSchema accepts only plan in {ESSENTIALS,COMPLETE} and interval in {monthly,yearly}; an unsupported interval/plan is rejected by the schema.
testTitle: billing checkout accepts only supported plan intervals
gapDescription: The schema is unit-tested in isolation (safeParse), but no test proves the route wires it so that POST /billing/checkout with a malformed body returns 400 {code:'VALIDATION_ERROR'} (the index.ts ZodError catch branch) rather than a 500 or an uncaught throw. Also the unsupported-plan branch is not exercised, only unsupported-interval.
proposedTitle: POST /checkout with an invalid plan returns 400 VALIDATION_ERROR and never calls Stripe
proposedApproach: Boot Fastify, decorate prisma with authModels(OWNER, activeSubscription) plus an organisation.findUniqueOrThrow spy, register billingRoutes with prefix /billing, inject POST /billing/checkout with an OWNER bearer token and payload {plan:'PRO',interval:'monthly'}; assert 400 and json().code==='VALIDATION_ERROR' and that the organisation.findUniqueOrThrow / Stripe create mocks were NOT called.

guarantee: A webhook event whose checkout metadata is missing/invalid (no organisationId, unrecognised plan, or no subscription id) is rejected with 400 STRIPE_WEBHOOK_MISMATCH and performs no subscription write.
testTitle: checkout.session.completed rejects metadata when the customer or price does not match the organisation plan

guarantee: POST /billing/checkout (and /create-checkout) reject a MEMBER and an ADMIN with 403 {code:'FORBIDDEN'} via requireOwner, and the BillingService createCheckoutSession (organisation.findUniqueOrThrow / Stripe) is never invoked.
gapDescription: requireOwner is correctly attached as a preHandler in index.ts, but no test boots billingRoutes through HTTP with an authed prisma to prove a non-OWNER role is rejected. route-guards.test.ts proves requireAdmin/requireOwner semantics only for board-member and team routes, not billing.
proposedTitle: checkout and portal require OWNER (MEMBER and ADMIN are rejected with FORBIDDEN)
proposedApproach: Reuse the route-guards.test.ts idiom: decorate prisma with authModels(role, activeSubscription) plus a checkout-session spy; for role in [MEMBER, ADMIN] inject POST /billing/checkout and /billing/portal with tokenFor(role); assert 403 and json().code==='FORBIDDEN' and that the Stripe checkout/portal create spy was NOT called.

guarantee: POST /billing/portal (and /create-portal) reject a MEMBER and an ADMIN with 403 FORBIDDEN via requireOwner before createPortalSession runs.
gapDescription: Same as checkout: requireOwner is wired but unproven at the HTTP boundary for the portal routes.
proposedTitle: checkout and portal require OWNER (MEMBER and ADMIN are rejected with FORBIDDEN)
proposedApproach: Covered by the same test as the checkout authz row: include /billing/portal and /billing/create-portal in the injected route list and assert 403 FORBIDDEN with no billingPortal.sessions.create call.

guarantee: All authed billing routes (/checkout, /create-checkout, /portal, /create-portal, /status) reject an unauthenticated request with 401 {code:'UNAUTHORIZED'} via the authGuard onRequest hook, before any service call.
gapDescription: The authGuard hook is registered on the authed sub-scope, but no test injects these routes without a token to confirm 401. The only billing HTTP-level tests target the unauthenticated webhook route.
proposedTitle: authed billing routes reject unauthenticated requests with 401
proposedApproach: Boot billingRoutes with a prisma whose authSession.findFirst/user.findUnique are spies; inject each of POST /billing/checkout, /create-checkout, /portal, /create-portal and GET /billing/status with no authorization header; assert every response is 401 json().code==='UNAUTHORIZED' and the BillingService Stripe spies were never called.

guarantee: GET /billing/status returns the subscription for the caller's own organisationId only: getStatus queries subscription.findUnique({where:{organisationId: request.user.organisationId}}), so an OWNER of org A can never observe org B's plan/status/access.
testTitle: billing status only allows past-due access inside the grace window
gapDescription: The grace-window test calls getStatus('org_1') directly and proves the access computation, but does not prove the route passes request.user.organisationId (not a client-supplied/foreign id) into the query, i.e. that the query is keyed by the authenticated org. No HTTP-level test confirms the org scoping is taken from the token.
proposedTitle: GET /status scopes the subscription lookup to the caller's organisation
proposedApproach: Decorate prisma with authModels(OWNER) for organisationId 'org-1' and a subscription.findUnique spy that records its where arg; inject GET /billing/status with tokenFor(OWNER); assert the recorded where.organisationId === 'org-1' (the token org), proving the lookup is keyed by request.user.organisationId rather than any client input.

guarantee: A checkout.session.completed webhook can only attach a subscription to the organisation that owns the matching stripeCustomerId: if the session/subscription customer id does not equal organisation.stripeCustomerId, the handler throws 400 STRIPE_WEBHOOK_MISMATCH and performs no subscription.upsert (preventing an attacker-controlled session from binding a subscription to a foreign org).
testTitle: checkout.session.completed rejects metadata when the customer or price does not match the organisation plan

guarantee: customer.subscription.updated and .deleted only mutate the local subscription whose stripeSubscriptionId matches and whose organisation.stripeCustomerId matches the event's customer; a mismatched customer id throws 400 STRIPE_WEBHOOK_MISMATCH (via verifyExistingSubscription) and an unknown stripeSubscriptionId returns without writing.
testTitle: subscription.updated maps unhandled Stripe statuses to an access-denying status
gapDescription: The updated-path happy case (status mapping) is proven, but no test exercises verifyExistingSubscription's customer-mismatch rejection on the updated/deleted paths, nor the 'existing is null -> no write' early return for an unknown stripeSubscriptionId. The customer-mismatch guard is only proven on the checkout path.
proposedTitle: subscription.updated and .deleted reject a customer that does not own the local subscription and ignore unknown subscription ids
proposedApproach: Using billingHarness, (a) set existingSubscription.organisation.stripeCustomerId='cus_org_1' but deliver an updated/deleted event with customer 'cus_attacker' and assert.rejects(/does not match/) with no subscription.update call; (b) set existingSubscription=null and assert handleWebhook resolves with no subscription.update call.

guarantee: When STRIPE_SECRET_KEY/STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET/price ids are unconfigured, checkout, portal and the webhook signature step fail with a clean AppError 503 {code:'BILLING_NOT_CONFIGURED'} (handled to a 503 response) rather than a 500 or stack leak.
testTitle: missing Stripe webhook signatures return a client error
gapDescription: 503 BILLING_NOT_CONFIGURED is thrown by getStripe/getPriceId/constructWebhookEvent in source, but no test asserts the 503 status+code surfaces through any endpoint when Stripe env is absent. The signature test environment sets STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET, so the unconfigured 503 branch is unexercised.
proposedTitle: checkout returns 503 BILLING_NOT_CONFIGURED when Stripe is unconfigured
proposedApproach: With STRIPE_SECRET_KEY unset/placeholder, boot billingRoutes with authModels(OWNER, activeSubscription) and an organisation.findUniqueOrThrow returning an org; inject POST /billing/checkout with an OWNER token and valid body; assert 503 and json().code==='BILLING_NOT_CONFIGURED' and that no stripe.checkout.sessions.create occurred (real getStripe used, so it throws before any Stripe call).

guarantee: GET /billing/status degrades softly when billing is unconfigured: it returns 200 with billingConfigured:false (and hasAccess derived from stored subscription) instead of a 503, so the status endpoint keeps working when Stripe env is absent.
