gapDescription: getStatus calls isConfigured() and returns billingConfigured without throwing, but no test asserts that an unconfigured Stripe env yields a 200 status payload with billingConfigured:false rather than an error.
proposedTitle: billing status reports billingConfigured:false without erroring when Stripe is unconfigured
proposedApproach: With Stripe price/secret env vars deleted, instantiate new BillingService(prisma) where subscription.findUnique returns null; call getStatus('org_1') and assert the resolved object equals {plan:null,...,hasAccess:false,billingConfigured:false} (no throw, no 503).

guarantee: BillingService.isConfigured() truthfully reflects Stripe readiness (all of secret key, webhook secret, and every plan price id configured) and feeds the readiness endpoint's billingConfigured check, so readiness reports not_ready when billing secrets are missing.
testTitle: unauthenticated readiness does not expose detailed dependency checks
gapDescription: The readiness route consumes billing.isConfigured() (routes/health/index.ts line 62/70), but the only readiness test asserts billingConfigured is hidden from unauthenticated callers; no test asserts isConfigured() returns false when a single required price id is missing, nor that authenticated readiness flips to 503/not_ready on that basis.
proposedTitle: isConfigured returns false when any required Stripe price id is missing
proposedApproach: Instantiate new BillingService(prisma); with all four price ids plus secret+webhook set assert isConfigured()===true, then delete STRIPE_COMPLETE_YEARLY_PRICE_ID and assert isConfigured()===false, proving each price id is required for the readiness signal.

guarantee: plan-gating (requireCompletePlan / subscriptionGuard blocking expired tenants) does not apply to the billing route group itself.
gapDescription: The billing endpoints are intentionally NOT behind subscriptionGuard or requireCompletePlan: checkout/portal/status must remain reachable precisely when a subscription is absent, expired, or past-due so an owner can pay or fix billing. Gating them would deadlock recovery. (The subscriptionGuard/grace-window tests in this file prove gating for OTHER route groups, not billing.) Webhook->plan correctness is covered separately by the checkout price/metadata mismatch test.

guarantee: auth-session rotation/replay does not apply to the billing group; the webhook route is unauthenticated (signature-verified) and the authed routes rely on the shared authGuard with no billing-specific token handling, and the service logs neither raw bodies, signatures, nor secrets.
gapDescription: Refresh-token rotation/replay is owned by the auth route group, not billing. The only billing logging is app.log.warn({code,statusCode}) / app.log.error(err) in the webhook catch (index.ts lines 36-39), which logs the AppError code and status but not the raw body or stripe-signature, so there is no billing-specific secret-logging guarantee to prove here.

guarantee: GET /compliance-record for an org whose subscription.plan is ESSENTIALS (or anything other than COMPLETE) never invokes loadGovernanceRegisters: none of conflictRecord/riskRecord/complaintRecord/fundraisingRecord/annualReportReadiness/financialControlReview findMany/findUnique are called, and the response body contains no 'Governance registers' heading nor any register data.
testTitle: Essentials exports do not include Complete-only governance registers

guarantee: GET /compliance-record for an org whose subscription.plan is COMPLETE DOES read and embed the governance registers: loadGovernanceRegisters runs and the response body includes the 'Governance registers' heading plus the org's conflict/risk/complaint/fundraising rows.
gapDescription: The negative case (ESSENTIALS excluded) is proven, but no test proves the positive path — that a COMPLETE plan actually surfaces the registers. Without it the gate could silently exclude registers for everyone (a false-negative regression) and the existing test would still pass.
proposedTitle: Complete plan exports include governance registers
proposedApproach: Decorate the prisma mock as in the CSP test but with subscription.findUnique returning {status:'ACTIVE', plan:'COMPLETE'} and conflictRecord.findMany returning one row (e.g. trusteeName 'Visible trustee'); inject GET /compliance-record?year=2026 with the ADMIN authHeader; assert statusCode 200 and response.body matches /Governance registers/ and /Visible trustee/.

guarantee: An org with no active subscription cannot reach the export at all: subscriptionGuard returns 403 {code:'NO_SUBSCRIPTION'} before the handler runs, and organisation.findUniqueOrThrow / the compliance reads are never executed.
gapDescription: subscriptionGuard is registered on the export plugin but no test exercises it against an export URL. NO_SUBSCRIPTION/TRIAL_EXPIRED/PAST_DUE_GRACE_EXPIRED/SUBSCRIPTION_INACTIVE rejection on these two endpoints is unproven; the existing tests only ever use a valid trial/active subscription.
proposedTitle: export rejects organisation with no subscription
proposedApproach: Decorate prisma with valid authSession+verified user but subscription.findUnique returning null, and spy on organisation.findUniqueOrThrow; inject GET /compliance-record?year=2026 with the ADMIN authHeader; assert statusCode 403, JSON code 'NO_SUBSCRIPTION', and that the organisation.findUniqueOrThrow spy was NOT called.

guarantee: An org whose trial has expired (status TRIALING, trialEndsAt in the past) is blocked from the export with 403 {code:'TRIAL_EXPIRED'} and the report HTML is never built.
gapDescription: No test drives the export route with an expired-trial subscription; the TRIAL_EXPIRED branch of subscriptionGuard is uncovered for export endpoints.
proposedTitle: export rejects organisation with expired trial
proposedApproach: Decorate prisma with subscription.findUnique returning {status:'TRIALING', trialEndsAt: new Date(Date.now()-60_000)} and spy on organisation.findUniqueOrThrow; inject GET /compliance-record?year=2026; assert statusCode 403, code 'TRIAL_EXPIRED', and organisation.findUniqueOrThrow spy NOT called.

guarantee: The report is always built for request.user.organisationId only: organisation.findUniqueOrThrow is called with where{id: request.user.organisationId} and the compliance principle/record/signoff/subscription reads are scoped to that same id, so a caller authenticated as org A can never render org B's organisation header, RCN, principles, records or signoff.
gapDescription: The handler reads organisationId solely from the JWT-derived request.user (no client-supplied org id), but no test asserts that the org id passed to organisation.findUniqueOrThrow and to the ComplianceService methods equals the token's organisationId rather than any attacker-influenced value. The CSP/registers tests use a single org and never check the scoping argument.
proposedTitle: export scopes the organisation and compliance reads to the token organisation
proposedApproach: Capture the where argument in organisation.findUniqueOrThrow and complianceRecord.findMany; sign the authHeader for organisationId 'org-A' and inject GET /compliance-record?year=2026; assert the captured organisation where.id === 'org-A' and the complianceRecord findMany where.organisationId === 'org-A' (proving no foreign org id reaches the queries).

guarantee: On the COMPLETE plan, every governance-register read in loadGovernanceRegisters is scoped to request.user.organisationId: conflictRecord/riskRecord/complaintRecord/fundraisingRecord findMany are called with where{organisationId}, and annualReportReadiness/financialControlReview findUnique with where{organisationId_reportingYear:{organisationId, reportingYear}}, so a COMPLETE org A export can never embed org B's registers.
gapDescription: The ESSENTIALS test proves the registers are not read at all on the wrong plan, but for a COMPLETE org no test asserts that each of the six register queries is filtered by the token's organisationId (rather than fetching all orgs' rows). Cross-org leakage of register data on the COMPLETE plan is unproven.
proposedTitle: Complete plan governance registers are scoped to the token organisation
proposedApproach: With subscription plan 'COMPLETE' and authHeader for 'org-A', capture the where arg of conflictRecord/riskRecord/complaintRecord/fundraisingRecord.findMany and annualReportReadiness/financialControlReview.findUnique; inject GET /compliance-record?year=2026; assert each findMany where.organisationId === 'org-A' and each findUnique where.organisationId_reportingYear.organisationId === 'org-A'.

guarantee: A request with no/invalid/expired token, a revoked session, or a missing user is rejected by authGuard with 401 {code:'UNAUTHORIZED'} before the export handler runs; an authenticated but email-unverified user is rejected with 403 {code:'EMAIL_NOT_VERIFIED'}.
gapDescription: authGuard is registered on the export plugin but no test injects an export URL without a token, with a forged/expired token, with authSession.findFirst returning null (revoked), or with user.emailVerified=false. These 401/403 boundaries are proven generically elsewhere but not on /compliance-record or /compliance-report.
proposedTitle: export rejects missing token, revoked session, and unverified email
proposedApproach: Three injects against GET /compliance-record?year=2026 with a write/handler spy on organisation.findUniqueOrThrow: (a) no authorization header -> 401 UNAUTHORIZED; (b) prisma.authSession.findFirst returns null -> 401 UNAUTHORIZED; (c) user.findUnique returns emailVerified:false -> 403 EMAIL_NOT_VERIFIED; in all three assert organisation.findUniqueOrThrow spy NOT called.

guarantee: GET /compliance-record with a malformed, missing, out-of-range, or oversized year (e.g. year=abc, year omitted, year=1999, year=99999) returns 400 {code:'VALIDATION_ERROR'} (never a 500 or stack leak) and the report HTML / organisation lookup is never reached for the invalid value.
gapDescription: All existing export tests pass year=2026. The ZodError catch branch returning 400 VALIDATION_ERROR is never exercised, so a regression that let a bad year through to a 500 or to organisation.findUniqueOrThrow would not be caught.
proposedTitle: export rejects malformed year with 400 VALIDATION_ERROR
proposedApproach: Using the valid prisma mock from the CSP test plus a spy on organisation.findUniqueOrThrow, inject GET /compliance-record?year=abc with the ADMIN authHeader; assert statusCode 400, parsed JSON code 'VALIDATION_ERROR', no 'Internal server error', and that organisation.findUniqueOrThrow spy was NOT called. Repeat for year=1999 (below min) and a missing year.

guarantee: User-supplied compliance/register text (e.g. standard titles, actionTaken, evidence, explanationIfNA, trusteeName, matter, mitigation, complaint summary, controls) is HTML-escaped via escapeHtml/simpleTable before being embedded in the exported report, so a stored '<script>' or '"' in a record cannot break out of its cell or inject markup into the generated HTML.
gapDescription: The exported document is text/html and interpolates stored record/register strings; escapeHtml is applied but no test proves a malicious stored value (e.g. actionTaken containing '<script>alert(1)</script>') is rendered escaped rather than as live markup. Note status values from recordMap (line 159) are inserted without escapeHtml — a test would pin down exactly which fields are escaped.
proposedTitle: export HTML-escapes stored record and register values
proposedApproach: Mock complianceRecord.findMany / governancePrinciple.findMany so a standard title or actionTaken contains '<script>alert(1)</script>' and (COMPLETE plan) a conflictRecord matter contains the same; inject GET /compliance-record?year=2026; assert response.body contains '<script>' and does NOT contain the raw '<script>alert(1)</script>'.

guarantee: The export does not depend on Stripe/Supabase/Resend at request time (no checkout, storage, or email call), so it stays available when those externals are down; conversely if the org row is missing, organisation.findUniqueOrThrow surfaces a clean error via handleError (404/500) rather than leaking a stack.
gapDescription: The export handler makes only Prisma reads — it never calls Stripe, Supabase storage, or Resend — so the 503-on-external-outage concern has no surface here. The only error path is Prisma findUniqueOrThrow / unexpected errors, which handleError maps to a safe INTERNAL_ERROR 500 without exposing the message in production (covered conceptually by errors.ts behaviour). No export-specific external-degradation test is warranted.

guarantee: GET /api/v1/dashboard/ with no Authorization header (or an invalid/expired token) returns 401 {code:'UNAUTHORIZED'} and never reaches the aggregate reads; a revoked session token is likewise rejected 401.
gapDescription: No test boots dashboardRoutes. route-guards.test.ts proves authGuard 401 behaviour for board-member and team routes but never registers the dashboard plugin, so the dashboard group's own onRequest authGuard wiring is unproven.
proposedTitle: dashboard route rejects unauthenticated requests
proposedApproach: Boot Fastify({logger:false}), app.decorate('prisma', {authSession:{findFirst:async()=>null}, ...}) and register dashboardRoutes with prefix '/'. inject({method:'GET', url:'/'}) with no headers; assert statusCode 401 and json().code === 'UNAUTHORIZED'. Assert no aggregate read mock (deadline.findMany / boardMember.findMany) was called.

guarantee: GET /api/v1/dashboard/ for an authenticated user whose email is unverified returns 403 {code:'EMAIL_NOT_VERIFIED'} and performs none of the aggregate reads.
gapDescription: auth-isolation.test.ts proves authGuard emits 403 EMAIL_NOT_VERIFIED at the middleware level, but no test exercises that path through the dashboard plugin.
proposedTitle: dashboard route rejects users with an unverified email
proposedApproach: Decorate prisma with authSession.findFirst -> {id:'sess-1'} and user.findUnique -> {id:'u1',organisationId:'org-1',role:'OWNER',emailVerified:false}. signAccessToken({userId,organisationId,role,sessionId}) as Bearer; inject GET '/'. Assert 403 and code 'EMAIL_NOT_VERIFIED'; assert deadline.findMany/boardMember.findMany mocks not called.

guarantee: GET /api/v1/dashboard/ for an organisation with no subscription returns 403 {code:'NO_SUBSCRIPTION'}; an expired trial returns 403 {code:'TRIAL_EXPIRED'}; an ended past-due grace returns 403 {code:'PAST_DUE_GRACE_EXPIRED'}; an otherwise-inactive subscription returns 403 {code:'SUBSCRIPTION_INACTIVE'} — in every case before any aggregate read runs.
gapDescription: subscriptionGuard is wired as the second onRequest hook on the dashboard group, but no test registers dashboardRoutes to prove it. route-guards.test.ts proves NO_SUBSCRIPTION only for the board-member route.
proposedTitle: dashboard route requires an active subscription
proposedApproach: Decorate prisma with a verified user and authSession ok, but subscription.findUnique -> null. inject GET '/' with a valid Bearer token; assert 403 and code 'NO_SUBSCRIPTION'. Add cases for trialEndsAt in the past (TRIAL_EXPIRED) and status 'PAST_DUE' (PAST_DUE_GRACE_EXPIRED). Assert deadline.findMany / boardMember.findMany were not called.

guarantee: GET /api/v1/dashboard/ scopes the inline upcoming-deadlines read to the caller's org: deadline.findMany is invoked with where.organisationId === request.user.organisationId (plus isComplete:false and dueDate.gte), so deadlines of another org can never appear in upcomingDeadlines.
gapDescription: The deadline.findMany call lives inline in the route handler (not in a service) and no test boots the route, so the organisationId filter on this query is unasserted.
proposedTitle: dashboard scopes upcoming deadlines to the requesting organisation
proposedApproach: Boot the route with a valid OWNER token for organisationId 'org-1'. Decorate prisma.deadline.findMany to capture its args and return []. inject GET '/'; assert the captured where.organisationId === 'org-1' (and where.isComplete === false). Use a no-op ComplianceService-backing prisma (governanceStandard.findMany [], complianceRecord.findMany [], organisation.findUniqueOrThrow {complexity:'SIMPLE'}, subscription.findUnique {plan:'ESSENTIALS'}).

guarantee: GET /api/v1/dashboard/ scopes the inline board-members read (which drives boardAlerts) to the caller's org: boardMember.findMany is invoked with where.organisationId === request.user.organisationId, so no foreign-org member can surface as a conduct_unsigned / induction_pending / term_expiring alert.
gapDescription: boardMember.findMany is called inline in the route handler and consumed into boardAlerts; no test boots the route, so the organisationId scoping of the alert source is unproven.
proposedTitle: dashboard scopes board-alert source members to the requesting organisation
proposedApproach: Boot the route with an OWNER token for 'org-1'. Make prisma.boardMember.findMany capture args; assert where.organisationId === 'org-1'. Return one member with conductSigned:false to confirm the alert is built only from the org-scoped query result; assert the response boardAlerts reference only that member's id.

guarantee: The compliance summary embedded in the dashboard is org-scoped: ComplianceService.getSummary filters complianceRecord.findMany by where.organisationId === the caller's org and resolves the org's complexity/plan via organisation.findUniqueOrThrow({id:organisationId}) and subscription.findUnique({organisationId}), so another org's records never affect the dashboard compliance figures.
testTitle: getSummary excludes NOT_APPLICABLE from the denominator and computes per-principle percentages
gapDescription: Existing getSummary tests prove the aggregation math but use a hand-rolled prisma that returns the same records regardless of args; they never assert that complianceRecord.findMany is called with where.organisationId equal to the org argument. So org-scoping of the compliance aggregate is not falsifiably proven.
proposedTitle: getSummary scopes the compliance-record query to the requested organisation
proposedApproach: Instantiate new ComplianceService(prismaMock) where complianceRecord.findMany captures its args and organisation.findUniqueOrThrow/subscription.findUnique return scope. Call service.getSummary('org-1', 2026) and assert the captured where.organisationId === 'org-1' and where.reportingYear === 2026.

guarantee: Every per-resource query inside ActivityService.getRecentActivity is scoped to the caller's org: complianceRecord.findMany, document.findMany, boardMember.findMany and deadline.findMany are each invoked with where.organisationId === the org argument, and the org's plan/complexity are resolved via organisation.findUniqueOrThrow({id:organisationId}) / subscription.findUnique({organisationId}); so no foreign-org compliance update, document upload, board-member change or deadline change can appear in recentActivity.
testTitle: Essentials recent activity excludes additional-standard compliance records
gapDescription: The existing ActivityService test proves only the plan-based standard-scope (isCore vs additional) of the compliance branch; its mocks return fixed arrays and assert nothing about organisationId on any of the four findMany calls, so cross-org isolation of the activity feed is unproven.
proposedTitle: recent activity scopes every source query to the requested organisation
proposedApproach: Instantiate new ActivityService(prismaMock) where each of complianceRecord/document/boardMember/deadline.findMany captures its args and returns []. Call getRecentActivity('org-1', 10); assert every captured where.organisationId === 'org-1' and that organisation.findUniqueOrThrow was called with where.id === 'org-1' and subscription.findUnique with where.organisationId === 'org-1'.

guarantee: ActivityService.getRecentActivity includes additional (non-core) compliance standards in the feed only when the org is COMPLEX AND on the COMPLETE plan; otherwise it scopes complianceRecord.findMany to standard.isCore:true, so an ESSENTIALS org never sees additional-standard compliance activity on its dashboard.
testTitle: Essentials recent activity excludes additional-standard compliance records

guarantee: GET /api/v1/dashboard/ surfaces an internal failure (e.g. a rejected prisma read) as a controlled error response via handleError rather than leaking a stack trace or returning an unhandled 500 with internals.
gapDescription: The handler wraps its Promise.all in try/catch -> handleError, but no test exercises a failing dependency through the dashboard route to confirm a clean, code-bearing error body with no stack leak.
proposedTitle: dashboard route returns a clean error when an aggregate read fails
proposedApproach: Boot the route with a valid authenticated/subscribed prisma but make deadline.findMany reject with a generic Error. inject GET '/'; assert the response is a 4xx/5xx JSON body produced by handleError (has an 'error'/'code' shape) and that the body contains no stack trace text. Confirms the catch path, not an unhandled rejection.

guarantee: Concern does not apply: GET /api/v1/dashboard/ accepts no request body and no validated path or query parameters (organisationId and year are derived server-side from request.user and new Date()), so there is no client-supplied input to validate or reject.

guarantee: The org-scoped record lookup keys on BOTH id and organisationId (findFirst where {id, organisationId}), so an id alone cannot select another org's record.

guarantee: listConflicts/listRisks/listComplaints/listFundraising each issue findMany with where {organisationId} so a caller only ever sees their own org's records (no global list leak).
testTitle: summary counts only non-closed records scoped to the organisation
gapDescription: summary() proves the count() where-clause carries organisationId, but no test asserts the four list* findMany calls pass where {organisationId}. The list methods are a distinct read path from count and are currently unproven.
proposedTitle: list register methods scope findMany to the caller organisation
proposedApproach: Using buildService(), call listConflicts/listRisks/listComplaints/listFundraising('org_1') and assert each recorded <model>.findMany call has args.where deep-equal {organisationId:'org_1'}.

guarantee: summary() counts conflicts/risks/complaints/fundraising with where {organisationId, status:{not:'CLOSED'}}, so the dashboard totals never include another org's records.
testTitle: summary counts only non-closed records scoped to the organisation

guarantee: getAnnualReportReadiness / upsertAnnualReportReadiness only ever address rows via the compound unique organisationId_reportingYear, so org A can never read or overwrite org B's annual-report readiness row.
testTitle: getAnnualReportReadiness returns a safe default shape when no record exists
gapDescription: The default-shape test proves findUnique is used but does not assert the where key includes the caller's organisationId, and no test covers upsert's where/create payload carrying organisationId. Cross-org isolation here rests on the compound key but is not asserted.
proposedTitle: annual report readiness reads and upserts are scoped to organisationId_reportingYear
proposedApproach: Extend buildService to capture annualReportReadiness.findUnique/upsert args; call getAnnualReportReadiness('org_1',2026) and upsertAnnualReportReadiness('org_1',{reportingYear:2026,...}); assert the where is {organisationId_reportingYear:{organisationId:'org_1',reportingYear:2026}} and the create payload sets organisationId:'org_1'.

guarantee: getFinancialControlReview / upsertFinancialControlReview only ever address rows via the compound unique organisationId_reportingYear, so org A can never read or overwrite org B's financial-controls row.
gapDescription: No test asserts the financial-controls findUnique/upsert where carries the caller's organisationId or that the create payload sets organisationId. Isolation rests entirely on the compound key with no regression guard.
proposedTitle: financial control review reads and upserts are scoped to organisationId_reportingYear
proposedApproach: Capture financialControlReview.findUnique/upsert args in buildService; call getFinancialControlReview('org_1',2026) and upsertFinancialControlReview('org_1',{reportingYear:2026,...}); assert where is {organisationId_reportingYear:{organisationId:'org_1',reportingYear:2026}} and the upsert create sets organisationId:'org_1'.

guarantee: POST /conflicts by a MEMBER is rejected 403 FORBIDDEN by requireAdmin and conflictRecord.create is never called.
gapDescription: requireAdmin is wired on POST /conflicts but no governance-registers HTTP test drives a MEMBER token through it; only board-member routes prove requireAdmin (route-guards.test.ts).
proposedTitle: a MEMBER cannot create conflict records (requireAdmin)
proposedApproach: Boot Fastify, decorate prisma with authSession.findFirst->{id}, user.findUnique->{role:'MEMBER',emailVerified:true,organisationId:'org-1'}, subscription.findUnique->{status:'ACTIVE',plan:'COMPLETE'}, and a conflictRecord.create spy. Register governanceRegisterRoutes at prefix '/governance-registers'; inject POST /governance-registers/conflicts with a MEMBER Bearer token and valid body; assert 403, code FORBIDDEN, and the create spy was NOT called.

guarantee: PATCH /conflicts/:id and DELETE /conflicts/:id by a MEMBER are rejected 403 FORBIDDEN by requireAdmin without reaching conflictRecord.update/delete.
gapDescription: No HTTP test exercises requireAdmin on the conflict PATCH/DELETE endpoints.
proposedTitle: a MEMBER cannot update or delete conflict records (requireAdmin)
proposedApproach: With the COMPLETE-plan MEMBER prisma mock above plus conflictRecord.update/delete spies, inject PATCH /governance-registers/conflicts/c1 and DELETE /governance-registers/conflicts/c1 with a MEMBER token; assert each is 403 FORBIDDEN and neither update nor delete spy was called.

guarantee: POST /risks, PATCH /risks/:id, DELETE /risks/:id by a MEMBER are rejected 403 FORBIDDEN by requireAdmin without any riskRecord write.
gapDescription: requireAdmin on the risk write endpoints is untested at the HTTP level.
proposedTitle: a MEMBER cannot write risk records (requireAdmin)
proposedApproach: Reuse the COMPLETE-plan MEMBER mock with riskRecord.create/update/delete spies; inject POST /governance-registers/risks, PATCH /governance-registers/risks/r1, DELETE /governance-registers/risks/r1 with a MEMBER token; assert each 403 FORBIDDEN and no risk write spy fired.

guarantee: POST /complaints, PATCH /complaints/:id, DELETE /complaints/:id by a MEMBER are rejected 403 FORBIDDEN by requireAdmin without any complaintRecord write.
gapDescription: requireAdmin on the complaint write endpoints is untested at the HTTP level.
proposedTitle: a MEMBER cannot write complaint records (requireAdmin)
proposedApproach: Reuse the COMPLETE-plan MEMBER mock with complaintRecord.create/update/delete spies; inject the three complaint write routes with a MEMBER token; assert 403 FORBIDDEN each and no complaint write spy fired.

guarantee: POST /fundraising, PATCH /fundraising/:id, DELETE /fundraising/:id by a MEMBER are rejected 403 FORBIDDEN by requireAdmin without any fundraisingRecord write.

guarantee: PUT /annual-report and PUT /financial-controls by a MEMBER are rejected 403 FORBIDDEN by requireAdmin without reaching annualReportReadiness.upsert / financialControlReview.upsert.
gapDescription: requireAdmin on the two upsert write endpoints is untested at the HTTP level.

guarantee: An ADMIN (the minimum allowed write role) is permitted through requireAdmin on a register write (e.g. POST /risks returns 201), confirming the guard is not over-restrictive.
gapDescription: No positive-path HTTP test confirms an ADMIN may write to any governance register; only the negative MEMBER path would be added above.

guarantee: An ESSENTIALS-plan org is blocked 403 PLAN_FEATURE_UNAVAILABLE on the register READ endpoints other than summary (GET /conflicts, /risks, /complaints, /fundraising, /annual-report, /financial-controls), with no findMany/findUnique reaching the service.
testTitle: Essentials organisations cannot access Complete-only governance register endpoints
gapDescription: requireCompletePlan is registered once for the whole plugin, but the existing test only exercises GET /summary. The other six read endpoints are not individually asserted to be plan-gated.
proposedTitle: Essentials org is blocked from every governance register read endpoint
proposedApproach: Boot Fastify with an ESSENTIALS subscription mock and read spies on conflictRecord.findMany/annualReportReadiness.findUnique etc.; loop GET over /conflicts,/risks,/complaints,/fundraising,/annual-report?year=2026,/financial-controls?year=2026 with an OWNER token; assert every response is 403 PLAN_FEATURE_UNAVAILABLE and no read spy fired.

guarantee: An ESSENTIALS-plan org is blocked 403 PLAN_FEATURE_UNAVAILABLE on every register WRITE endpoint (POST/PATCH/DELETE conflicts/risks/complaints/fundraising, PUT annual-report, PUT financial-controls), with no create/update/delete/upsert reaching the service.
gapDescription: No test confirms plan-gating fires on the write endpoints; requireCompletePlan runs as a preHandler before requireAdmin, so an ESSENTIALS write must 403 with PLAN_FEATURE_UNAVAILABLE (not FORBIDDEN), but this ordering is unproven.
proposedTitle: Essentials org is blocked from every governance register write endpoint
proposedApproach: With an ESSENTIALS subscription mock and write spies, inject each write route (POST /conflicts, PATCH /risks/r1, DELETE /complaints/c1, PUT /annual-report, etc.) using an OWNER token and valid bodies; assert each is 403 with code PLAN_FEATURE_UNAVAILABLE and no write spy fired.

proposedTitle: POST /conflicts rejects malformed body with 400 VALIDATION_ERROR and no write
proposedApproach: Boot Fastify with a COMPLETE-plan ADMIN mock and a conflictRecord.create spy; inject POST /governance-registers/conflicts with an empty body (and separately dateDeclared:'31-12-2026'); assert 400, code VALIDATION_ERROR, and the create spy was NOT called.

guarantee: POST /risks with likelihood/impact outside 1-5 (or a non-enum category) returns 400 VALIDATION_ERROR and riskRecord.create is never called.

proposedTitle: POST /risks with out-of-range likelihood returns 400 VALIDATION_ERROR and no write
proposedApproach: With a COMPLETE-plan ADMIN mock and a riskRecord.create spy, inject POST /governance-registers/risks with {title,category:'FINANCIAL',description,likelihood:6,impact:4,mitigation}; assert 400, code VALIDATION_ERROR, and the create spy was NOT called.

gapDescription: complianceQuerySchema.parse(request.query) is caught and mapped to 400 VALIDATION_ERROR, but no test exercises a bad year query string through the route.

proposedApproach: With a COMPLETE-plan mock, inject GET /governance-registers/summary?year=notanumber with an OWNER token; assert 400 and code VALIDATION_ERROR (no 500, no count spy invoked).

guarantee: PUT /annual-report and PUT /financial-controls with reportingYear outside 2018-2100 (or wrong type) reject with 400 VALIDATION_ERROR and the upsert is never called.
gapDescription: upsertAnnualReportReadinessSchema/upsertFinancialControlReviewSchema bound reportingYear to 2018-2100, but no HTTP test proves the route returns 400 VALIDATION_ERROR and skips the upsert for an out-of-range year.
proposedTitle: PUT /annual-report rejects an out-of-range reportingYear and never upserts
proposedApproach: With a COMPLETE-plan ADMIN mock and an annualReportReadiness.upsert spy, inject PUT /governance-registers/annual-report with {reportingYear:1900}; assert 400, code VALIDATION_ERROR, and the upsert spy was NOT called.

guarantee: PUT /annual-report accepts a valid governance-workflow body (reportingYear + narrative/booleans/filingStatus + ISO boardApprovalDate) without a validation error.

guarantee: Every governance-registers endpoint requires a valid access token: a request with no/invalid token is rejected 401 UNAUTHORIZED by authGuard before any service call.

proposedTitle: governance register routes require authentication
proposedApproach: Boot Fastify with governanceRegisterRoutes and a prisma mock; inject GET /governance-registers/summary with no authorization header; assert 401 and code UNAUTHORIZED.

guarantee: POST /invites from a MEMBER is rejected with 403 FORBIDDEN and never creates a teamInvite (ensureCanInvite).

testTitle: the owner cannot promote a member to OWNER (no silent owner transfer)

guarantee: DELETE /invites/:id from a MEMBER is rejected with 403 FORBIDDEN and never calls teamInvite.findFirst or teamInvite.update (revoke calls ensureCanInvite(actorRole)).

guarantee: DELETE /invites/:id on an already-accepted invite is rejected with 400 INVITE_ACCEPTED and never calls teamInvite.update.
gapDescription: No test covers the acceptedAt branch of revoke; only the cross-tenant 404 path is proven.
proposedTitle: revoke rejects an already-accepted invite
proposedApproach: new TeamService({teamInvite:{findFirst: async()=>({id:'inv_1',organisationId:'org_1',acceptedAt:new Date()}), update}}, noopEmail). Call service.revoke('org_1','inv_1','OWNER'); assert rejects with code 'INVITE_ACCEPTED' and that the update mock was NOT called.

guarantee: Auth-gated team endpoints (GET /, POST /invites, DELETE /invites/:id, PATCH /members/:id/role) are blocked with 403 (NO_SUBSCRIPTION / TRIAL_EXPIRED / PAST_DUE_GRACE_EXPIRED / SUBSCRIPTION_INACTIVE) when the organisation has no active/within-grace subscription (subscriptionGuard).
testTitle: board-member routes require an active subscription
gapDescription: The subscriptionGuard is registered on the authed team sub-app, but the NO_SUBSCRIPTION/expired-subscription 403 is only proven on the board-member route group, not on any team endpoint; team tests always supply an active or valid trialing subscription.
proposedTitle: authenticated team routes require an active subscription
proposedApproach: Extend buildTeamApp to accept a subscription arg and decorate prisma.subscription.findUnique to return null. Inject GET / with a valid OWNER Bearer token and assert statusCode 403 and json().code === 'NO_SUBSCRIPTION'.

guarantee: POST /invites on an ESSENTIALS organisation that is at the 5-member limit (members + pending invites) is rejected with 403 TEAM_MEMBER_LIMIT_EXCEEDED and never creates the invite or sends an email; COMPLETE plans have no limit.
testTitle: Essentials organisations cannot invite beyond the team member limit

guarantee: POST /accept-invite for a stale invite is rejected with 403 TEAM_MEMBER_LIMIT_EXCEEDED when the ESSENTIALS member limit is already reached, without consuming the invite or creating the user (assertCanAddTeamMember inside the transaction).
testTitle: acceptInvite rejects stale invites when the Essentials member limit is already reached

guarantee: invite/accept abort with 403 NO_SUBSCRIPTION when the organisation has no Subscription row at the point of the member-capacity check (assertCanAddTeamMember).

gapDescription: subscriptionGuard already blocks the authed /invites path before TeamService runs, but acceptInvite is public and reaches assertCanAddTeamMember directly; its NO_SUBSCRIPTION branch (subscription.findUnique returns null) is never exercised by a test.
proposedTitle: acceptInvite rejects when the organisation has no subscription

guarantee: POST /accept-invite with a malformed body (missing token, empty name, or a password failing the min-length/uppercase/lowercase/digit rules) returns 400 VALIDATION_ERROR via formatZodError, never a 500 or stack leak.

guarantee: POST /invites with a malformed body (invalid email, or role not in {ADMIN,MEMBER}) returns 400 VALIDATION_ERROR and never calls teamInvite.create.

proposedApproach: Build a team app with a valid OWNER token and active subscription; spy on teamInvite.create. Inject POST /invites with payload {email:'not-an-email', role:'MEMBER'}; assert statusCode 400, json().code === 'VALIDATION_ERROR', and that the create mock was NOT called.

guarantee: PATCH /members/:id/role with a malformed body (role not in {OWNER,ADMIN,MEMBER}) returns 400 VALIDATION_ERROR and never calls user.update.
