proposedApproach: With a valid OWNER token + active subscription and a user.update spy, inject PATCH /members/m1/role with payload {role:'SUPERADMIN'}; assert statusCode 400, json().code === 'VALIDATION_ERROR', and that user.update was NOT called.

guarantee: POST /invites returns 202 with the neutral accepted message even when the Resend-backed EmailService.sendTeamInvite rejects, because the email is dispatched fire-and-forget (void) after the invite transaction commits.
gapDescription: Invite tests assert email is NOT sent (suppressed) cases and a successful-send case, but none proves the endpoint still succeeds (202 + accepted message, invite already created) when sendTeamInvite throws/rejects.
proposedTitle: invite still succeeds when the email provider fails
proposedApproach: new TeamService(prismaMock-where-create-succeeds, {sendTeamInvite: async () => { throw new Error('resend down'); }}). Call service.invite('org-1','owner-1','OWNER',{email:'x@example.org',role:'MEMBER'}) and assert it resolves to {message: TEAM_INVITE_ACCEPTED_MESSAGE} (the create mock was called), confirming the rejected fire-and-forget email does not surface to the caller.

guarantee: POST /accept-invite issues fresh session tokens and sets auth cookies (201) only after the invite is atomically consumed; if consumption loses the race (updateMany count 0) or the user-email uniqueness check races, no session is issued and the response is 400 INVALID_INVITE.
testTitle: acceptInvite binds the new user to the role and organisation from the INVITE, not the request

guarantee: Auth-gated team endpoints (GET /, POST /invites, PATCH /members/:id/role) reject unauthenticated requests with 401 UNAUTHORIZED, while POST /accept-invite is reachable without auth (never 401) and validates the token to 400 INVALID_INVITE.
testTitle: authenticated team routes reject unauthenticated requests

guarantee: The invite token is stored and matched only as a hashed opaque value (hashOpaqueToken), and acceptInvite rejects invites whose state is missing/accepted/revoked/expired with 400 INVALID_INVITE without revealing the cause.
testTitle: acceptInvite rejects a missing token

guarantee: Re-inviting an email that already has an active (non-expired, non-revoked, non-accepted) invite or that already belongs to a user is a no-op: no new invite is created, no email is sent, and the same neutral 202 message is returned; a DB unique-constraint race (P2002) on the active-invite index is also flattened to the same accepted response.
testTitle: team invites do not disclose why an invite recipient is unavailable

guarantee: GET /api/v1/health/readiness with a valid x-charitypilot-readiness-key returns 200 {status:'ready'} and checks={database:true, billingConfigured:true, emailConfigured:true, storageConfigured:true, storageBucketReachable:true} ONLY when every one of the five dependency probes resolves truthy.
gapDescription: No test exercises the authorized happy path. health-readiness.test.ts only proves the 401 leak guard and the database-timeout 503 path; the 200/ready aggregation (all five checks true => ready=true => status 200 'ready') is never asserted, so a regression that drops or inverts any of the five conjuncts in the `ready` expression would go undetected.
proposedTitle: authenticated readiness reports ready when every dependency is healthy
proposedApproach: Set READINESS_API_KEY and all of STRIPE_SECRET_KEY/STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET/the four STRIPE_*_PRICE_ID, RESEND_API_KEY, EMAIL_FROM, plus DOCUMENT_STORAGE_DRIVER='local' (so StorageService.isConfigured and verifyBucket both succeed without Supabase). Decorate prisma with $queryRaw async ()=>[{result:1}], inject GET /api/v1/health/readiness with header x-charitypilot-readiness-key set to the configured key, assert statusCode 200, body.status==='ready', and that every field of body.checks is true. Restore env in finally.

guarantee: When the database probe fails (prisma.$queryRaw throws or hangs past READINESS_DEPENDENCY_TIMEOUT_MS), readiness returns 503 {status:'not_ready', checks.database:false} rather than a 500 or a hang, and the error is logged without crashing the request.
testTitle: authenticated readiness times out a stuck database check

guarantee: When the database probe REJECTS (prisma.$queryRaw throws synchronously/asynchronously, e.g. connection refused) rather than hanging, readiness still returns 503 with checks.database:false and never propagates the error as a 500/stack leak.
testTitle: authenticated readiness times out a stuck database check
gapDescription: The timeout (never-resolving promise) branch is proven, but the catch branch on a rejected query (the try/catch at index.ts lines 45-50 that sets database=false and logs) is not exercised. A thrown error and a hang are distinct code paths; only the hang is currently tested.
proposedTitle: authenticated readiness reports not_ready when the database query throws
proposedApproach: Set READINESS_API_KEY, decorate prisma with $queryRaw async ()=>{ throw new Error('connection refused'); }, inject GET /api/v1/health/readiness with the valid key header, assert statusCode 503, body.status==='not_ready', body.checks.database===false, and that the response JSON contains no Error message/stack substring.

guarantee: When Stripe (billing), Resend (email), or Supabase (storage) are unconfigured/unreachable, readiness reports the corresponding check as false and returns 503 not_ready, while the public liveness probe GET /api/v1/health continues to return 200 (the app keeps serving).
testTitle: basic health stays public without exposing dependency checks
gapDescription: Liveness staying up (200) is proven and the database-down 503 is proven, but no test proves the readiness aggregation flips to 503 specifically because billingConfigured / emailConfigured / storageConfigured / storageBucketReachable is false while the database is healthy. The per-dependency contribution of Stripe/Resend/Supabase to the not_ready verdict is unproven.
proposedTitle: authenticated readiness reports not_ready when billing/email/storage are unconfigured
proposedApproach: Set READINESS_API_KEY, decorate prisma with a healthy $queryRaw, and DELETE STRIPE_SECRET_KEY / RESEND_API_KEY / SUPABASE_URL (leave DOCUMENT_STORAGE_DRIVER unset so storage is Supabase-mode and unconfigured). Inject GET readiness with the valid key, assert statusCode 503, body.status==='not_ready', and body.checks.database===true while billingConfigured/emailConfigured/storageConfigured/storageBucketReachable are false. Restore env in finally.

guarantee: GET /api/v1/health/readiness returns 401 {code:'READINESS_UNAUTHORIZED'} and exposes none of the dependency check field names when no readiness key header is supplied (with READINESS_API_KEY configured).
testTitle: unauthenticated readiness does not expose detailed dependency checks

guarantee: GET /api/v1/health/readiness returns 401 {code:'READINESS_UNAUTHORIZED'} when the supplied x-charitypilot-readiness-key header is present but does NOT match the configured READINESS_API_KEY (wrong-key rejection), without running or exposing the dependency probes.
gapDescription: The existing leak test sends NO header (missing key). The distinct mismatch path — a present-but-wrong header that must fail the timingSafeStringEqual comparison — is never asserted, so a comparison regression (e.g. accidentally returning true) could pass undetected.
proposedTitle: readiness rejects a wrong readiness key without exposing checks
proposedApproach: Set READINESS_API_KEY='readiness-test-secret', build the health app, inject GET /api/v1/health/readiness with header x-charitypilot-readiness-key:'wrong-key', assert statusCode 401, body.code==='READINESS_UNAUTHORIZED', and that JSON.stringify(body) contains neither 'billingConfigured' nor 'storageBucketReachable'. Restore env in finally.

guarantee: GET /api/v1/health/readiness returns 401 {code:'READINESS_UNAUTHORIZED'} when READINESS_API_KEY is unset or a placeholder (isConfiguredSecret false) even if a header is supplied — readiness can never be reached on an instance with no configured key.
gapDescription: hasReadinessAccess returns false early when isConfiguredSecret(configuredKey) is false (index.ts lines 19-20). No test deletes READINESS_API_KEY while supplying a header to prove an unconfigured instance fails closed; a regression that defaulted to 'open when no key is set' would not be caught.
proposedTitle: readiness fails closed when no readiness key is configured
proposedApproach: delete process.env.READINESS_API_KEY, build the health app, inject GET /api/v1/health/readiness with header x-charitypilot-readiness-key:'any-value', assert statusCode 401, body.code==='READINESS_UNAUTHORIZED', and absence of 'checks' in body. Restore the original key in finally.

guarantee: The readiness key comparison is timing-safe (SHA-256 digest + crypto.timingSafeEqual), never a plain === string comparison, so the gate is not vulnerable to timing side channels.
testTitle: readiness key comparison uses timing-safe equality

guarantee: Neither the supplied nor the configured readiness key value is ever echoed into the response body or logged in cleartext; the 401 response carries only a generic error/code and dependency-probe failures are logged without the key.
testTitle: unauthenticated readiness does not expose detailed dependency checks
gapDescription: The 401 body is proven to not leak check field names, but no test asserts that the readiness key secret itself is absent from the response body or from emitted log lines. The route logs probe errors via request.log.error (index.ts lines 49,56) — there is no assertion that the key never appears in any log output.
proposedTitle: readiness never echoes the readiness key in the response body
proposedApproach: Set READINESS_API_KEY to a distinctive sentinel, inject GET readiness with a wrong header value (also a distinctive sentinel), assert statusCode 401 and that JSON.stringify(body) contains neither the configured key sentinel nor the supplied header sentinel; optionally capture a Fastify logger stream to assert the key sentinel is absent from log output.

guarantee: GET /api/v1/health (liveness) is public, takes no input, returns 200 {status:'ok', timestamp:string} and never includes a `checks` field, so it leaks no dependency detail to anonymous callers.
testTitle: basic health stays public without exposing dependency checks

guarantee: A malformed/duplicated x-charitypilot-readiness-key header (e.g. sent as an array of values, which Fastify exposes as a non-string) is rejected with 401 {code:'READINESS_UNAUTHORIZED'} rather than throwing a 500, because hasReadinessAccess requires typeof suppliedKey === 'string' before the timing-safe compare.
gapDescription: The typeof string guard (index.ts line 23) is the only input-validation on this route and is untested. A duplicated header would arrive as string[] and, absent the guard, would crash timingSafeStringEqual with a 500; no test pins this to a clean 401.
proposedTitle: readiness rejects a duplicated readiness key header without a 500
proposedApproach: Set READINESS_API_KEY, build the health app, inject GET /api/v1/health/readiness with headers providing x-charitypilot-readiness-key as an array (e.g. ['a','b']) so Fastify yields a non-string value, assert statusCode 401 and body.code==='READINESS_UNAUTHORIZED' (not 500) and that 'checks' is absent.

guarantee: Production env validation rejects a missing or too-short READINESS_API_KEY (must be >=32 chars and configured) so a weak/absent readiness gate can never reach production.
testTitle: validateProductionEnv rejects missing production readiness key

guarantee: N/A — neither health endpoint reads or mutates any organisation-scoped resource; the readiness probe only runs an unparameterised SELECT 1 and config/reachability checks, so there is no organisationId surface and no cross-org leak is possible.
gapDescription: No request.user, no organisationId, and no Prisma model query that returns tenant data — the only DB call is prisma.$queryRaw`SELECT 1`. There is nothing for an org-A user to read of org B here.

guarantee: N/A — health and readiness are infrastructure probes deliberately outside the subscription/plan model; they carry no requireCompletePlan or subscriptionGuard and must remain reachable regardless of any tenant's plan state.
gapDescription: The routes are registered with no auth/subscription/plan guards (server.ts line 81 registers healthRoutes with only a prefix). Gating readiness behind a subscription would defeat its purpose, so plan-gating does not apply.

guarantee: N/A — both endpoints are side-effect-free GET probes (no writes, no webhook ingestion, no reminder/dedupe logic), so there is no operation that must be processed exactly once.
gapDescription: Readiness only reads (SELECT 1, getBucket, config flags). StorageService.verifyBucket may mkdir a local dir under the local driver, but that call is already idempotent (recursive:true) and unrelated to a once-only business operation.

guarantee: verifyAccessToken rejects a token signed with any algorithm other than the pinned HS256 (e.g. an HS384 token signed with the same JWT_SECRET throws 'invalid algorithm'), and signAccessToken emits alg=HS256.
testTitle: access tokens are signed and verified with the pinned HS256 algorithm

guarantee: verifyAccessToken rejects access tokens that are not bound to issuer 'charitypilot-api' AND audience 'charitypilot-web' (wrong-audience token and missing-issuer token both throw), and signAccessToken stamps iss/aud.
testTitle: access tokens are bound to the CharityPilot issuer and API audience

guarantee: authGuard rejects with 401 {code:'UNAUTHORIZED'} when the JWT is structurally valid but its backing AuthSession lookup (id+userId, revokedAt:null, expiresAt>now) returns null (session revoked/expired), and does not populate request.user.
testTitle: authGuard rejects access tokens whose backing session has been revoked

guarantee: authGuard derives request.user (organisationId, role) from the live DB user row rather than from stale JWT claims, so a token minted with an outdated org/role is downgraded to the current values.
testTitle: authGuard uses current database role and organisation instead of stale JWT claims

guarantee: authGuard returns 403 {code:'EMAIL_NOT_VERIFIED'} for a valid session whose user is unverified, while authIdentityGuard (allowUnverified) lets the same unverified user through and sets request.user.
testTitle: authGuard rejects valid sessions for users whose email is not verified

guarantee: authGuard returns 401 {code:'UNAUTHORIZED'} when no bearer token and no access-token cookie is present on the request.
gapDescription: No test exercises the no-token branch of authenticateRequest (request with neither an Authorization header nor an access cookie). The revoked-session and stale-claim tests both supply a valid signed token.
proposedTitle: authGuard rejects requests with no bearer token and no access cookie
proposedApproach: Call authGuard with a request object {headers:{}, cookies:{}, server:{prisma:{authSession:{findFirst},user:{findUnique}}}} where both prisma mocks throw if called; assert reply.statusCode===401, payload {error:'Missing or invalid authentication token', code:'UNAUTHORIZED'}, request.user undefined, and that neither prisma lookup ran.

guarantee: authGuard reads the access token from the HttpOnly cookie (ACCESS_TOKEN_COOKIE) via getAccessTokenFromRequest when no Authorization bearer header is present, so cookie-based sessions authenticate identically to bearer sessions.
gapDescription: Every existing authGuard test supplies the token via the Authorization header; the cookie fallback path (token = bearerToken ?? getAccessTokenFromRequest(request)) is never exercised.
proposedTitle: authGuard authenticates from the access-token cookie when no bearer header is present
proposedApproach: signAccessToken a valid payload, place it in request.cookies under ACCESS_TOKEN_COOKIE (import from utils/auth-cookie-names.js), leave headers empty, mock authSession.findFirst -> {id} and user.findUnique -> verified user; assert reply.statusCode===200 and request.user is populated.

guarantee: Successful refresh rotation atomically revokes the presented session and creates exactly one replacement session whose refreshTokenHash is the SHA-256 of a NEW random token (not the old hash), returning a fresh access token bound to the new sessionId.
testTitle: refresh token rotation fails without creating a replacement session when revoke loses the race
gapDescription: Only the two FAILURE paths of rotateSessionTokens are proven (revoke loses the race -> no replacement; replay -> revoke-all). The happy path is unproven: no test asserts that on a valid, unrevoked, unexpired session the old row is revoked AND a new session is created with a DIFFERENT refreshTokenHash and that the returned refreshToken differs from the input.
proposedTitle: refresh token rotation revokes the old session and issues a new session with a fresh hash
proposedApproach: Mock prisma.$queryRaw -> [{id:'s1',userId:'u1',expiresAt:future,revokedAt:null}], user.findUnique -> {id:'u1',organisationId:'o1',role:'OWNER'}, and $transaction(cb) running cb(tx) where tx.authSession.updateMany returns {count:1} (capture its where to assert it targets id:'s1'+revokedAt:null) and tx.authSession.create captures data.refreshTokenHash. Call rotateSessionTokens(prisma,'old-token'); assert the returned refreshToken !== 'old-token', that hashOpaqueToken(returned) === the captured create hash, that the captured hash !== hashOpaqueToken('old-token'), and that accessToken verifyAccessToken's sessionId matches the new session id.

guarantee: A replayed (already-revoked) refresh token is rejected with 401 INVALID_REFRESH_TOKEN and triggers a revoke-all of the user's active sessions, without re-looking-up the user or creating a replacement session.
testTitle: refresh token replay revokes active sessions for the affected user

guarantee: A refresh token whose hash matches no AuthSession row, and a token whose session is past expiresAt, are both rejected with 401 INVALID_REFRESH_TOKEN without creating a replacement session.
gapDescription: rotateSessionTokens has three distinct early-reject branches: no row ($queryRaw -> []), revoked row (covered), and expired row (expiresAt <= now). The no-row and expired-but-not-revoked branches have no test.
proposedTitle: refresh rotation rejects unknown and expired refresh tokens without minting a session
proposedApproach: Two cases sharing a prisma mock whose authSession.create throws if called: (a) $queryRaw -> [] ; (b) $queryRaw -> [{id:'s1',userId:'u1',expiresAt:new Date(Date.now()-1000),revokedAt:null}] with user.findUnique throwing if reached. assert.rejects rotateSessionTokens with AppError 401 INVALID_REFRESH_TOKEN in both, and assert create was never invoked.

guarantee: AuthService.login issues an access token + opaque refresh token and creates exactly one AuthSession (storing the SHA-256 hash, never the raw token) only after bcrypt.compare succeeds against the stored hash.
gapDescription: Only the no-user / wrong-password failure paths of login are tested (auth-login-timing.test.ts). The success path — valid credentials produce tokens and persist a session with a hashed (not raw) refresh token — is unproven.
proposedTitle: login issues tokens and persists a session with the hashed refresh token on valid credentials
proposedApproach: Build a real bcrypt hash of 'GoodPass1' via bcryptjs; mock prisma.user.findUnique -> {id:'u1',organisationId:'o1',role:'OWNER',passwordHash:hash,emailVerified:true,organisation:{...}} and authSession.create capturing data.refreshTokenHash. new AuthService(prisma, emailSpy).login({email,password:'GoodPass1'}); assert result.accessToken/refreshToken are non-empty strings, hashOpaqueToken(result.refreshToken) === captured hash, and the captured hash !== result.refreshToken (raw token never stored).

guarantee: AuthService.logout revokes only the AuthSession matching the presented refresh-token hash (revokedAt IS NULL filter) and never throws when the token is absent/unknown.
gapDescription: revokeSessionToken / AuthService.logout has no test. Nothing proves it scopes the UPDATE to the presented hash (rather than the user's other sessions) or that it is a no-op for an unknown token.
proposedTitle: logout revokes only the session for the presented refresh token
proposedApproach: Mock prisma.$executeRaw to capture the tagged-template values; call new AuthService(prisma,emailSpy).logout('tok'); assert the captured parameter list includes hashOpaqueToken('tok') (so the WHERE targets that single hash) and that the call resolves to {message:'Signed out successfully.'}.

guarantee: AuthService.resetPassword consumes the reset token atomically (updateMany count must be 1) and, in the same transaction, revokes ALL of the user's active sessions (authSession.updateMany where userId, revokedAt:null); a concurrently-consumed token rejects 400 INVALID_RESET_TOKEN without revoking sessions.
testTitle: resetPassword consumes the token, sets the password, and revokes all sessions

guarantee: AuthService.verifyEmail is single-use: it flips emailVerified only when the atomic updateMany affects exactly one row, and rejects 400 INVALID_VERIFY_TOKEN (without an unconditional update) when the token is missing or already consumed.
testTitle: verifyEmail consumes the verification token atomically

guarantee: AuthService.login does not leak account existence: the no-account path spends a full bcrypt compare (timing parity) and returns the same generic 401 INVALID_CREDENTIALS 'Invalid email or password' as a wrong-password attempt.
testTitle: login spends bcrypt hashing time even when the email has no account

guarantee: The /auth/refresh route falls back to the HttpOnly refresh cookie when no body token is given, returns 401 INVALID_REFRESH_TOKEN when no token is present at all, and clears auth cookies on any refresh failure (so a rejected/replayed token does not leave a stale cookie).
gapDescription: No HTTP-level test injects against /auth/refresh. The cookie-fallback, missing-token 401, and clearAuthCookies-on-error behaviours in routes/auth/index.ts are unproven. (governance-workflow.test.ts only validates the refreshSchema shape, not the route.)
proposedTitle: refresh route clears cookies and returns 401 when the refresh token is invalid
proposedApproach: Boot Fastify({logger:false}), register @fastify/cookie then authRoutes at /auth, decorate prisma with $queryRaw -> [] (unknown token). inject POST /auth/refresh with a refresh cookie; assert statusCode 401 code INVALID_REFRESH_TOKEN and that the set-cookie header expires/clears the refresh + access cookies (maxAge 0).

guarantee: setAuthCookies marks both the access and refresh cookies HttpOnly, SameSite=lax, path=/, Secure in production, with maxAge 15m (access) and the configured refresh TTL; clearAuthCookies expires both.
gapDescription: utils/auth-cookies.ts has no test. The Secure-only-in-production, HttpOnly, SameSite=lax, and maxAge attributes that protect the session cookies against XSS/CSRF exfiltration are entirely unverified.
proposedTitle: auth cookies are HttpOnly, SameSite=lax, and Secure in production
proposedApproach: Boot a Fastify app with @fastify/cookie and one route that calls setAuthCookies(reply,{accessToken:'a',refreshToken:'r'}); with NODE_ENV='production' inject a request and parse the set-cookie headers, asserting each contains HttpOnly, SameSite=Lax, Secure, Path=/, and Max-Age. Repeat with NODE_ENV unset to assert Secure is absent, and a clearAuthCookies route to assert Max-Age=0.

guarantee: The production logger redacts Authorization/Cookie/Set-Cookie/stripe-signature headers and password/token/accessToken/refreshToken/refreshTokenHash body+top-level fields, and serializeErrorForLog scrubs raw tokens (token=...), emails, and storage paths from error messages while emitting a [redacted] stack — so credentials and session tokens never reach logs.
testTitle: production logger redacts browser credentials, provider secrets, and auth tokens

guarantee: The JWT signing secret (env.JWT_SECRET) and other provider secrets are configured as redact paths so they are never serialized into log output.
testTitle: production logger redacts browser credentials, provider secrets, and auth tokens
gapDescription: The redaction test asserts env.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY is in the redact paths but does not assert env.JWT_SECRET (the secret that protects every access token) is present, even though it is listed in API_LOG_REDACT_PATHS. The session-relevant secret is therefore not pinned by a test.
proposedTitle: production logger redacts the JWT signing secret env path
proposedApproach: Extend the existing redact-paths assertions to add assert.ok(redact?.paths?.includes('env.JWT_SECRET')) and assert.ok(redact?.paths?.includes('refreshTokenHash')), locking the JWT secret and the stored refresh-hash into the redaction contract.

guarantee: BillingService.handleWebhook returns without retrieving the Stripe subscription, without creating a StripeWebhookEvent ledger row, and without any subscription mutation when an event id already exists in StripeWebhookEvent (replayed/redelivered event).
testTitle: duplicate Stripe webhook event ids are ignored before subscription mutation

guarantee: When two deliveries of the SAME (not-yet-recorded) Stripe event race past the pre-check and both enter the transaction, the second's stripeWebhookEvent.create raises P2002 and handleWebhook swallows it (returns) so the subscription is upserted at most once; a P2002 NOT on the ledger create (e.g. subscription.upsert) is rethrown.
testTitle: Stripe webhook unique errors outside the event ledger are rethrown
gapDescription: The rethrow arm (P2002 on subscription.upsert) is proven, but the swallow arm — a P2002 thrown by the in-transaction stripeWebhookEvent.create short-circuiting the whole webhook with NO subscription mutation — has no test. The existing duplicate test exercises the pre-transaction findUnique path, not the create-collision path at billing.service.ts:427-430.
proposedTitle: concurrent delivery of the same Stripe event aborts the second transaction when the ledger insert collides
proposedApproach: Use billingHarness with existingWebhookEvent:null and ledgerCreate that throws Object.assign(new Error('dup'),{code:'P2002'}); invoke handleWebhook for a checkout.session.completed event. Assert handleWebhook resolves (no throw) and that calls contains no 'subscription.upsert' entry, proving the ledger P2002 short-circuits before any subscription write.

guarantee: DeadlineRemindersService reserves the DeadlineReminderLog (unique deadlineId_email_reminderDays) before sending: on a fresh deadline it performs reserve -> send -> finalize in that order, so the email is only dispatched after the dedup row is claimed.
testTitle: deadline reminders reserve the unique reminder log before sending email

guarantee: When another worker has already reserved the same reminder window (reserveReminderLog.create throws P2002 and the existing row is SENT or a fresh non-reclaimable reservation), sendDueReminders skips: no email is sent and no finalize update runs.
testTitle: deadline reminders skip sending when another worker already reserved the reminder log

guarantee: A FAILED prior reminder row, or a stale (>15min) SKIPPED 'Reserved before delivery' reservation, is reclaimed exactly once (updateMany returns count===1) and re-sent; a non-stale or already-SENT row is not reclaimed.
testTitle: deadline reminders retry a previously failed reminder log instead of suppressing it
gapDescription: The happy reclaim paths (FAILED, and stale SKIPPED) are proven, but the negative guard — updateMany returning count!==1 (another worker reclaimed first) causing reservation to be null and the email NOT sent — is not tested, so the 'exactly one delivery wins the reclaim race' guarantee is only half proven.
proposedTitle: deadline reminders skip when a concurrent worker wins the reclaim race
proposedApproach: Mock deadlineReminderLog.create to throw P2002, findUnique to return a stale SKIPPED reservation (status 'SKIPPED', error 'Reserved before delivery', sentAt 30min ago), and updateMany to return {count:0}. Run sendDueReminders and assert operations === ['reserve','findUnique','reclaim'] with no 'send' and no 'finalize', proving a lost reclaim race suppresses the duplicate email.

guarantee: Reminder dedup keys on the configured window, not the live day count: if the exact trigger day was missed, the next run still sends the most urgent reached window and reserves DeadlineReminderLog.reminderDays = that window value (catch-up), and fires nothing before any window is reached.
testTitle: reminder catches up to the most urgent window when the exact day was missed

guarantee: Document deletion uses a transactional outbox: remove() enqueues a DocumentStorageDeletion row and deletes the document row in one $transaction (deletion record created at order 1, db delete at order 2), and if the db delete throws, storage is never deleted and the outbox row is never marked processed.
testTitle: document delete removes storage after deleting the database record

guarantee: retryPendingStorageDeletions processes each pending row at most once per success: on success it marks processedAt with lastError/claimedAt cleared (so a redelivery won't re-delete), and on storage failure it increments attempts and clears claimedAt leaving processedAt null so the row is retried safely on a later run rather than double-charged.
testTitle: retryPendingStorageDeletions processes pending cleanup records

guarantee: Concurrent cleanup runs never double-process a deletion: claimPendingStorageDeletions claims rows with a single UPDATE ... SET claimedAt ... WHERE processedAt IS NULL AND (claimedAt IS NULL OR claimedAt < now - 10min) ... FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED RETURNING ..., bound with the 600000ms stale window and limit, so a row already claimed by another worker within 10 minutes is skipped.
testTitle: retryPendingStorageDeletions atomically claims pending cleanup rows with Postgres row locks
gapDescription: The test asserts the SQL contains FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED and the bound values [600000, 10], proving the atomic-claim query shape. It does NOT assert the stale-claim predicate that lets a row whose claimedAt is older than 10 minutes be re-claimed (the 'claimedAt < CURRENT_TIMESTAMP - interval' WHERE clause), so reclaim-after-crash is unproven, and there is no behavioural test of two concurrent claimers skipping each other.
proposedTitle: claim query reclaims rows whose claim is older than the stale window
proposedApproach: Extend the $queryRaw mock harness to capture the full query string and assert.match it against /claimedAt" < CURRENT_TIMESTAMP - \(\?\s*\* INTERVAL '1 millisecond'\)/ and that the processedAt-null + claimedAt-null OR stale predicate is present, locking in the stale-reclaim arm of the WHERE clause (no write-mock interaction needed; this is a query-shape guarantee).

guarantee: When no Postgres row-locking is available ($transaction/$queryRaw absent, e.g. the standalone cron path), claimPendingStorageDeletions falls back to findMany({where:{processedAt:null},orderBy:{createdAt:'asc'},take:limit}) and still processes/marks each pending row exactly once.
testTitle: retryPendingStorageDeletions processes pending cleanup records

guarantee: A reminder or document-cleanup job failure fires exactly one sanitized error-alert webhook (method JOB, statusCode 500, correct url/code) with no raw secrets/PII, and runProductionSchedulerOnce reports the per-job failed flag; cleanup that returns failed>0 without throwing still alerts.
testTitle: runDeadlineReminders sends a sanitized operational alert when the production reminder run fails

guarantee: If the error-alert webhook sender itself throws while reporting a job failure, sendJobFailureAlert swallows the alerting error (logs it) so the scheduler does not crash and the run-failed signal is still returned.
gapDescription: production-scheduler.ts sendJobFailureAlert wraps alertSender in try/catch and only logs on failure (lines 181-185), but no test injects a throwing alertSender to prove a broken alert pipeline does not turn a recoverable job failure into an unhandled rejection.
proposedTitle: job failure alerting tolerates a throwing alert sender without crashing the run
proposedApproach: Call runDeadlineReminders with a deadlineService.sendDueReminders that throws and an alertSender that rejects; assert runDeadlineReminders resolves to true (failed) without rethrowing, and that the logger.error captured a '[ProductionScheduler] Failed to send deadline-reminders failure alert' message, proving alerting errors are contained.

guarantee: GET /api/v1/health (liveness) returns 200 {status:'ok', timestamp} and never exposes the dependency `checks` object to unauthenticated callers.
testTitle: basic health stays public without exposing dependency checks

guarantee: GET /api/v1/health/readiness rejects callers without the internal readiness key with 401 {code:'READINESS_UNAUTHORIZED'} and leaks no dependency detail (no `checks`, no billingConfigured/storageBucketReachable strings) in the body.
testTitle: unauthenticated readiness does not expose detailed dependency checks

guarantee: The readiness key is compared with constant-time equality (timingSafeEqual), never a plain === string compare, so the key is not vulnerable to timing analysis.
testTitle: readiness key comparison uses timing-safe equality
gapDescription: Proven by static source assertion (regex over routes/health/index.ts) rather than behaviorally, but the constant-time guarantee is structural and the source check is sufficient here.

guarantee: Readiness reflects real DB health: when the `SELECT 1` probe stalls past READINESS_DEPENDENCY_TIMEOUT_MS, readiness returns 503 {status:'not_ready'} with checks.database=false within the timeout budget rather than hanging.
testTitle: authenticated readiness times out a stuck database check

guarantee: Readiness reflects real DB health on the error path: when `app.prisma.$queryRaw\`SELECT 1\`` REJECTS (DB connection refused / query error, not a stall), the catch sets checks.database=false and readiness returns 503 {status:'not_ready'} without throwing a 500.
gapDescription: The only DB-failure readiness test covers a stalled (never-resolving) query via withReadinessTimeout; no test covers a $queryRaw that rejects synchronously/asynchronously with an error, which exercises the try/catch on lines 45-50 rather than the timeout race.
proposedTitle: authenticated readiness reports database:false when the SELECT 1 probe rejects
proposedApproach: Decorate prisma with `$queryRaw: async () => { throw new Error('connection refused'); }`, set READINESS_API_KEY, inject GET /api/v1/health/readiness with the x-charitypilot-readiness-key header, and assert statusCode 503, body.status 'not_ready', and body.checks.database === false (proving the catch on the rejected promise sets database false rather than surfacing a 500).

guarantee: Readiness returns 200 {status:'ready'} with all checks true only when the DB probe succeeds AND billing/email/storage are configured AND verifyBucket() succeeds — i.e. the all-green aggregation path produces a 200, not just the failure paths.
gapDescription: Every readiness test asserts a failure outcome (401, or 503 via stuck DB). No test drives the happy path where database=true and all isConfigured()/verifyBucket() checks are true and the endpoint returns 200 {status:'ready'}, so the `ready` aggregation (lines 68-73) and the 200 branch are unproven.
proposedTitle: authenticated readiness returns 200 ready when every dependency check passes
proposedApproach: Set READINESS_API_KEY and force local storage (DOCUMENT_STORAGE_DRIVER=local + LOCAL_FILE_STORAGE_DIR to a temp dir so storage.isConfigured() and verifyBucket() are true), configure billing+email secrets (STRIPE_SECRET_KEY / RESEND_API_KEY etc.) so their isConfigured() return true, decorate prisma with `$queryRaw: async () => [{result:1}]`, inject GET readiness with the key header, and assert statusCode 200, body.status 'ready', and every value in body.checks === true.

guarantee: Readiness returns 503 {status:'not_ready'} with the specific failing provider flag false (e.g. checks.storageBucketReachable=false or checks.emailConfigured=false) when an external provider is unconfigured/unreachable while the DB is healthy, so the probe distinguishes provider degradation from DB outage.
testTitle: verifyBucket returns false when the configured document bucket is public
gapDescription: The storage-service tests prove verifyBucket() returns false for a misconfigured/public bucket and that withReadinessTimeout returns null on a stalled dependency, but nothing proves the readiness ENDPOINT aggregates a healthy-DB-but-unhealthy-provider state into 503 with the correct provider flag false (e.g. storageBucketReachable=false, billingConfigured=false, or emailConfigured=false) while database stays true.
proposedTitle: authenticated readiness returns 503 with storageBucketReachable false when the bucket is unreachable
proposedApproach: Set READINESS_API_KEY, decorate prisma with a passing `$queryRaw` so checks.database=true, leave SUPABASE_* unset (or DOCUMENT_STORAGE_DRIVER unset) so storage.isConfigured()/verifyBucket() are false, inject readiness with the key header, and assert statusCode 503, body.status 'not_ready', body.checks.database === true, and body.checks.storageConfigured === false / storageBucketReachable === false.

guarantee: A request-path 5xx in production fires the error-alert webhook exactly once with a sanitized payload (service/method/url/statusCode/code/errorName/requestId/timestamp only; no headers, cookies, body, stack, message, query/cookie/payload secrets).
testTitle: production 5xx errors send a sanitized alert webhook payload

guarantee: Client (4xx) errors never fire the error-alert webhook (shouldSendErrorAlert returns false for statusCode<500).
testTitle: client errors do not send alert webhooks

guarantee: shouldSendErrorAlert suppresses the webhook when NODE_ENV is not 'production' OR when ERROR_ALERT_WEBHOOK_URL is unconfigured, even for a 5xx — so alerts are not fired in dev/test and not attempted when no webhook is configured.
gapDescription: All request-path alert tests set NODE_ENV='production' AND a configured webhook URL. The two suppression guards in shouldSendErrorAlert (NODE_ENV!=='production' short-circuit, and isConfiguredSecret(ERROR_ALERT_WEBHOOK_URL) short-circuit) are never exercised — a regression that always-on alerted regardless of env/config would pass the suite.
proposedTitle: 5xx errors do not send alerts outside production or when the webhook is unconfigured
proposedApproach: Build the error app, stub globalThis.fetch to flip a fetchCalled boolean, then (a) set NODE_ENV='development' with a configured ERROR_ALERT_WEBHOOK_URL and inject POST /boom asserting statusCode 500 and fetchCalled===false, and (b) set NODE_ENV='production' but delete ERROR_ALERT_WEBHOOK_URL and inject POST /boom asserting statusCode 500 and fetchCalled===false.

guarantee: A failed scheduled job actually fires the operational error-alert webhook: when DeadlineRemindersService.sendDueReminders() throws, runDeadlineReminders sends exactly one sanitized alert {method:'JOB', url:'/jobs/deadline-reminders', statusCode:500, code:'DEADLINE_REMINDERS_FAILED'} and returns failed=true, with no raw secrets in the payload or log.
testTitle: runDeadlineReminders sends a sanitized operational alert when the production reminder run fails

guarantee: A failed document-storage-cleanup job fires the operational alert: both when retryPendingStorageDeletions() throws and when it returns failed>0 without throwing, runDocumentStorageCleanup sends exactly one sanitized alert {url:'/jobs/document-storage-cleanup', code:'DOCUMENT_STORAGE_CLEANUP_FAILED'} and returns failed=true.
testTitle: runDocumentStorageCleanup sends a sanitized operational alert when storage cleanup fails

guarantee: A job that succeeds does NOT fire an alert: a clean runProductionSchedulerOnce (deadline reminders + cleanup with failed=0) returns {deadlineRemindersFailed:false, documentStorageCleanupFailed:false} and issues no error-alert webhook.
testTitle: runProductionSchedulerOnce runs reminders and document cleanup without overlapping API startup
gapDescription: The happy-path test asserts the success result and ordering but does not pass an alertSender spy nor assert that zero alerts were sent, so a regression that fired a spurious alert on a successful run would not be caught.
proposedTitle: runProductionSchedulerOnce fires no error alert when both jobs succeed
proposedApproach: Call runProductionSchedulerOnce with passing deadlineService/documentService (retryPendingStorageDeletions returns {processed:n, failed:0}) and pass an alertSender spy that pushes to an alerts[] array; assert the result has both *Failed flags false and that alerts.length === 0 (no webhook fired on success).

guarantee: Alert-webhook delivery failure during a job does not crash the job run: when sendErrorAlert (alertSender) itself rejects, sendJobFailureAlert swallows the error (logging it) and the run still returns failed=true rather than throwing.
gapDescription: The job-failure tests use an alertSender that always resolves. The sendJobFailureAlert try/catch around alertSender (production-scheduler lines 181-185) — which protects the run from a throwing alert transport — is never exercised in the scheduler suite. (error-alerts.test.ts ':: alert webhook failures do not change the API error response' proves this only for the request-path, not the job path.)
proposedTitle: runDeadlineReminders still returns failed when the alert sender throws
proposedApproach: Call runDeadlineReminders with a sendDueReminders that throws and an alertSender that itself throws ('alert destination unavailable'); assert the call resolves (does not reject), returns failed===true, and that a logger.error entry mentioning 'Failed to send deadline-reminders failure alert' was recorded.

guarantee: The alert webhook is hardened against abuse/hangs: it caps concurrent in-flight sends (ERROR_ALERT_WEBHOOK_MAX_IN_FLIGHT) and disallows redirects (redirect:'error').
testTitle: alert webhook sends are capped during error storms

guarantee: The alert webhook send is bounded in time: a slow/hung webhook is aborted after ERROR_ALERT_WEBHOOK_TIMEOUT_MS (AbortController) so a stuck alert endpoint cannot block the alert path indefinitely.
gapDescription: sendErrorAlert installs an AbortController + setTimeout(abort, alertTimeoutMs()) (lines 103-104, 121), but no test asserts the controller.signal is wired to the fetch call or that a never-resolving fetch is aborted within the configured timeout. The timeout/abort branch is unproven.
proposedTitle: alert webhook send aborts when the webhook hangs past the timeout
proposedApproach: Set ERROR_ALERT_WEBHOOK_URL and ERROR_ALERT_WEBHOOK_TIMEOUT_MS='25'; pass a fetchImpl that returns a promise rejecting only when its init.signal fires 'abort' (i.e. `new Promise((_,reject)=>signal.addEventListener('abort',()=>reject(new Error('aborted'))))`); call sendErrorAlert(payload, fetchImpl) and assert it rejects/settles within ~1s and that the signal was aborted, proving the timeout abort path is wired to fetch.

guarantee: When Supabase is unconfigured (SUPABASE_URL or SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY missing/placeholder), StorageService.getSupabaseClient() throws AppError(503, 'STORAGE_NOT_CONFIGURED') and no upload/signed-url/delete is attempted against Supabase.
testTitle: local storage driver writes, reads, signs, and deletes files without Supabase
gapDescription: The local-driver test proves storage works with Supabase env deleted, but no test asserts that with Supabase env unset AND the local driver disabled, uploadFile/getSignedUrl/deleteFile reject with status 503 and code STORAGE_NOT_CONFIGURED (the unconfigured-Supabase branch of getSupabaseClient is unproven).
proposedTitle: storage operations throw 503 STORAGE_NOT_CONFIGURED when Supabase is unconfigured and local driver is off
proposedApproach: Snapshot and delete DOCUMENT_STORAGE_DRIVER, SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY; new StorageService(); await assert.rejects for uploadFile('org-a','f.pdf',Buffer.from('x'),'application/pdf'), getSignedUrl('org-a','org-a/f.pdf'), deleteFile('org-a','org-a/f.pdf') each asserting err instanceof AppError, statusCode===503, code==='STORAGE_NOT_CONFIGURED'; restore env in finally.

guarantee: A failing Supabase API call on upload/signed-url/delete (provider returns an error object) is mapped to a stable AppError 500 with code STORAGE_UPLOAD_FAILED / STORAGE_SIGNED_URL_FAILED / STORAGE_DELETE_FAILED carrying a generic message, never leaking the raw provider error.
gapDescription: No test injects a Supabase client whose storage.from(...).upload/createSignedUrl/remove returns { error } and asserts the resulting AppError code/status; the error-mapping branches in uploadFile/getSignedUrl/deleteFile are unexercised.
proposedTitle: Supabase storage errors map to stable STORAGE_* AppErrors without leaking provider detail
proposedApproach: new StorageService() with Supabase env set to configured non-placeholder values and local driver off; monkeypatch the module-level Supabase client (or stub via a fake createClient) so from().upload returns {error:{message:'raw secret'}}; await assert.rejects(uploadFile(...)) asserting statusCode 500 and code STORAGE_UPLOAD_FAILED and that the message equals the generic STORAGE_OPERATION_FAILED_MESSAGE (not 'raw secret'). Repeat for createSignedUrl and remove.

guarantee: StorageService.verifyBucket() returns false (rather than throwing) when the bucket is public, when Supabase is unreachable/erroring, or when the readiness probe times out — so readiness degrades cleanly instead of crashing.
testTitle: verifyBucket returns false when the configured document bucket is public
gapDescription: The public-bucket false path and the generic withReadinessTimeout helper are proven, but there is no test that verifyBucket() itself returns false when getBucket rejects/throws (the catch branch) or when Supabase is entirely unconfigured (isConfigured()===false short-circuit).
proposedTitle: verifyBucket returns false when Supabase getBucket errors or is unconfigured
proposedApproach: Case A: point SUPABASE_URL at a local http server that returns 500 for /storage/v1/bucket/documents, assert (await service.verifyBucket())===false. Case B: delete SUPABASE_URL/SERVICE_ROLE_KEY/BUCKET with local driver off and assert verifyBucket()===false via the isConfigured() short-circuit; restore env in finally.

guarantee: EmailService._send never throws: when Resend is unconfigured (RESEND_API_KEY missing/placeholder or EMAIL_FROM has no '@') or the Resend SDK rejects, the send method returns false and the calling registration/auth/reminder flow proceeds.
gapDescription: email.service.test.ts only tests the happy path with a stubbed resend.emails.send that resolves; no test asserts that an unconfigured EmailService returns false without throwing, nor that a rejecting Resend send is caught and returns false (the two return-false branches in _send are unproven).
proposedTitle: _send returns false without throwing when Resend is unconfigured or the SDK rejects
proposedApproach: Case A: delete RESEND_API_KEY (or set to placeholder 're_...'); new EmailService(); assert (await service.sendWelcomeEmail('a@b.org','N','Org'))===false and that no send was attempted. Case B: configured key but stub resend.emails.send to throw an Error with a secret-bearing message; assert the call resolves to false (await assert.doesNotReject) and returns false; restore env in finally.

guarantee: When Stripe is unconfigured (STRIPE_SECRET_KEY / STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET / price IDs missing or placeholder), the billing endpoints (/checkout, /portal, /webhooks) surface a clean 503 BILLING_NOT_CONFIGURED via getStripe()/constructWebhookEvent()/getPriceId() and never mutate the subscription.
gapDescription: billing-reminders-hardening tests always run with Stripe env populated and getStripe() monkeypatched to a fake, so the unconfigured 503 branches in getStripe/getPriceId/constructWebhookEvent are never reached at HTTP level; no inject test proves /checkout or /webhooks returns 503 BILLING_NOT_CONFIGURED with no subscription write.
proposedTitle: billing webhook returns 503 BILLING_NOT_CONFIGURED when Stripe secret is unconfigured
proposedApproach: Snapshot then delete STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET (and STRIPE_SECRET_KEY); boot Fastify with prisma decorated so subscription.upsert/update are spies; register billingRoutes; inject POST /billing/webhooks with a 'stripe-signature' header and a Buffer body; assert response.statusCode===503 and json().code==='BILLING_NOT_CONFIGURED' and that no subscription write spy was called; restore env in finally.

guarantee: A document upload against an unconfigured Supabase returns a clean 503 STORAGE_NOT_CONFIGURED at the HTTP boundary (via handleError) rather than a 500 or stack leak, and no Document row is persisted.
gapDescription: The documents upload route calls storageService.uploadFile without a pre-check and relies on the thrown AppError(503) being surfaced by handleError; no inject-level test proves POST upload returns 503 STORAGE_NOT_CONFIGURED with the document.create mock NOT called when Supabase is unconfigured and the local driver is off.
proposedTitle: document upload returns 503 STORAGE_NOT_CONFIGURED and persists nothing when storage is unconfigured
proposedApproach: Build the documents app with env having DOCUMENT_STORAGE_DRIVER unset and SUPABASE_* deleted, and a document.create spy; inject a multipart POST upload with a valid auth header and small PDF buffer; assert statusCode===503, json().code==='STORAGE_NOT_CONFIGURED', and that the document.create spy was not called; restore env in finally.

guarantee: formatProviderError scrubs email addresses, token/signature query+fragment params, and storage paths and caps message length, so a provider failure logged anywhere (logger, document outbox, route logs) never leaks PII or secrets.
testTitle: retryPendingStorageDeletions leaves failed cleanup records pending with attempt metadata
gapDescription: Scrubbing is proven only indirectly through the document-deletion outbox (email + token + storage-path redaction asserted), but there is no direct unit test of formatProviderError covering the 160-char cap, the refresh_token/access_token fragment patterns, and the name/code/status assembly in isolation.
proposedTitle: formatProviderError redacts emails, tokens, storage paths and caps length
proposedApproach: Import formatProviderError; pass Object.assign(new Error('failed for a@b.com path org-1/x.pdf?token=abc #refresh_token=xyz '+'z'.repeat(200)),{code:'E',status:503}); assert the output matches name=Error, code=E, status=503, contains [email]/[storage-path]/token=[redacted]/refresh_token=[redacted], excludes a@b.com and abc and xyz, and the message segment is capped (ends with '...').

guarantee: The authenticated /readiness endpoint reflects real per-dependency health: it returns 503 not_ready (with the offending check flag false) when any single dependency — database, billing config, email config, storage config, or storage bucket reachability — is unhealthy, and 200 ready only when all pass.
testTitle: authenticated readiness times out a stuck database check
gapDescription: Only the database-timeout-to-503 path is proven. No test proves that a single unconfigured provider (e.g. billing or storage env missing) flips the aggregate to 503 not_ready with that specific check false while database stays true, nor the all-green 200 ready path.
proposedTitle: readiness reports 503 not_ready with billingConfigured false when Stripe env is unconfigured
proposedApproach: Set READINESS_API_KEY; decorate prisma so $queryRaw resolves [{result:1}]; delete STRIPE_SECRET_KEY/price IDs (and set storage local driver so storage checks pass) leaving billing unconfigured; inject GET /readiness with the readiness key header; assert statusCode===503, body.status==='not_ready', body.checks.database===true, body.checks.billingConfigured===false; restore env in finally.

guarantee: Invalid or missing Stripe webhook signatures are rejected with a 4xx client error (400 INVALID_STRIPE_SIGNATURE / MISSING_STRIPE_SIGNATURE) and never a 500, so a malformed/forged webhook degrades cleanly without mutating subscriptions.
testTitle: invalid Stripe webhook signatures return a client error
